72	    ReadPtxSregLanemaskEqOp, ReadPtxSregLanemaskGeOp, ReadPtxSregLanemaskGtOp,
73	    ReadPtxSregLanemaskLeOp, ReadPtxSregLanemaskLtOp, ReadPtxSregNsmIdOp, ReadPtxSregNtidXOp,
74	    ReadPtxSregNtidYOp, ReadPtxSregNwarpIdOp, ReadPtxSregSmIdOp, ReadPtxSregTidXOp,
75	    ReadPtxSregTidYOp, ReadPtxSregTotalSmemSizeOp, ReadPtxSregWarpIdOp,
76	};
77	use pliron::basic_block::BasicBlock;
78	use pliron::builtin::op_interfaces::OperandSegmentInterface;
79	use pliron::builtin::types::{IntegerType, Signedness};
80	use pliron::context::{Context, Ptr};
81	use pliron::identifier::Legaliser;
82	use pliron::linked_list::ContainsLinkedList;
83	use pliron::location::{Located, Location};
84	use pliron::op::Op;
85	use pliron::operation::Operation;
86	use pliron::r#type::Typed;
87	use pliron::{input_err, input_error};
88	use rustc_public::CrateDef;
89	use rustc_public::mir;
90	use rustc_public::ty::ConstantKind;
91	/// Translates a MIR terminator to Pliron IR control flow operation(s).
92	///
93	/// Handles all MIR terminator kinds:
94	/// - `Return`: Function return
95	/// - `Goto`: Unconditional branch
96	/// - `SwitchInt`: Multi-way branch (for enums, match)
97	/// - `Assert`: Runtime assertions with panic on failure
98	/// - `Call`: Function/intrinsic calls
99	/// - `Drop`: Destructor calls (no-op for Copy types)
100	/// - `Unreachable`: Marks unreachable code
101	///
102	/// # GPU Intrinsics
103	///
104	/// Calls to `cuda_device` functions are expanded inline to `dialect-nvvm` operations.
105	/// This includes thread indexing, synchronization, warp primitives, and
106	/// tensor core operations.
107	#[allow(clippy::too_many_arguments)]
108	pub fn translate_terminator(
109	    ctx: &mut Context,
110	    body: &mir::Body,
111	    term: &mir::Terminator,
112	    value_map: &mut ValueMap,
113	    block_ptr: Ptr<BasicBlock>,
114	    prev_op: Option<Ptr<Operation>>,
115	    block_map: &[Ptr<BasicBlock>],
116	    legaliser: &mut Legaliser,
117	) -> TranslationResult<Ptr<Operation>> {
118	    let loc = span_to_location(ctx, term.span);
119	
120	    match &term.kind {
121	        mir::TerminatorKind::Return => {
122	            translate_return(ctx, body, value_map, block_ptr, prev_op, loc)
123	        }
124	
125	        mir::TerminatorKind::Goto { target } => {
126	            translate_goto(ctx, *target, block_ptr, prev_op, block_map, loc)
127	        }
128	
129	        mir::TerminatorKind::Assert {
130	            cond,
131	            expected,
132	            msg: _,
133	            target,
134	            unwind,
135	        } => translate_assert(
136	            ctx, body, cond, *expected, *target, unwind, block_ptr, prev_op, value_map, block_map,
137	            loc,
138	        ),
139	
140	        mir::TerminatorKind::Call {
141	            func,
142	            args,
143	            destination,
144	            target,
145	            unwind,
146	        } => translate_call(
147	            ctx,
148	            body,
149	            func,
150	            args,
151	            destination,
152	            target,
153	            unwind,
154	            block_ptr,
155	            prev_op,
156	            value_map,
157	            block_map,
158	            loc,
159	            legaliser,
160	        ),
161	
162	        mir::TerminatorKind::SwitchInt { discr, targets } => translate_switch(
163	            ctx, body, discr, targets, block_ptr, prev_op, value_map, block_map, loc,
164	        ),
165	
166	        mir::TerminatorKind::Drop {
167	            place,
168	            target,
169	            unwind,
170	        } => translate_drop(
171	            ctx, body, place, *target, unwind, block_ptr, prev_op, block_map, loc,
172	        ),
173	
174	        mir::TerminatorKind::Unreachable => {
175	            // Create an unreachable operation
176	            let op = Operation::new(
177	                ctx,
178	                dialect_mir::ops::MirUnreachableOp::get_concrete_op_info(),
179	                vec![],
180	                vec![],
181	                vec![],
182	                0,
183	            );
184	            op.deref_mut(ctx).set_loc(loc);
185	            if let Some(prev) = prev_op {
186	                op.insert_after(ctx, prev);
187	            } else {
188	                op.insert_at_front(block_ptr, ctx);
189	            }
190	            Ok(op)
191	        }
192	
193	        _ => input_err!(
194	            loc.clone(),
195	            TranslationErr::unsupported(format!(
196	                "Terminator kind {:?} not yet implemented",
197	                term.kind
198	            ))
199	        ),
200	    }
201	}
202	
203	// ============================================================================
204	// Core Terminator Handlers
205	// ============================================================================
206	
207	/// Translates a MIR `Return` terminator to a `mir.return` operation.
208	///
209	/// Handles the return value (`_0`) from the function:
210	/// - For non-unit returns: passes the return value as an operand
211	/// - For unit returns (empty tuple): emits return with no operands
212	///
213	/// # MIR Semantics
214	///
215	/// In MIR, local 0 (`_0`) holds the return value. The return terminator
216	/// transfers control back to the caller with this value.
217	fn translate_return(
218	    ctx: &mut Context,
219	    body: &mir::Body,
220	    value_map: &mut ValueMap,
221	    block_ptr: Ptr<BasicBlock>,
222	    prev_op: Option<Ptr<Operation>>,
223	    loc: Location,
224	) -> TranslationResult<Ptr<Operation>> {
225	    // MIR local `_0` holds the return value. In the alloca + load/store model
226	    // we emit a `mir.load` from its slot to materialise the SSA value, then
227	    // pass it as the `mir.return` operand. ZSTs (including `()` kernel
228	    // returns) have no slot, so we simply emit a bare `return`.
229	    let return_local = mir::Local::from(0usize);
230	    let return_decl = &body.locals()[return_local];
231	    let return_type = types::translate_type(ctx, &return_decl.ty)?;
232	    let is_unit_return = {
233	        use dialect_mir::types::MirTupleType;
234	        let return_type_obj = return_type.deref(ctx);
235	        if let Some(tuple_ty) = return_type_obj.downcast_ref::<MirTupleType>() {
236	            tuple_ty.get_types().is_empty()
237	        } else {
238	            false
239	        }
240	    };
241	    let loaded = value_map.load_local(ctx, return_local, block_ptr, prev_op);
242	
243	    let (operands, terminator_prev_op) = match loaded {
244	        Some((load_op, val)) => {
245	            if is_unit_return {
246	                // Unit return: the load we just emitted is dead, but harmless;
247	                // leave it as prev_op so the return chains after it.
248	                (vec![], Some(load_op))
249	            } else {
250	                let (val, prev_op) =
251	                    maybe_ptr_coerce(ctx, val, return_type, block_ptr, Some(load_op));
252	                (vec![val], prev_op)
253	            }
254	        }
255	        None => {
256	            let return_ty = types::translate_type(ctx, &body.locals()[return_local].ty)?;
257	            let is_unit = return_ty
258	                .deref(ctx)
259	                .downcast_ref::<dialect_mir::types::MirTupleType>()
260	                .is_some_and(|tuple| tuple.get_types().is_empty());
261	            if types::is_zst_type(ctx, return_ty) && !is_unit {
262	                // Non-unit ZST returns still need a MIR-level value so the
263	                // `mir.return` verifier agrees with the function signature.
264	                // LLVM lowering erases the value and emits a void return.
265	                let undef = dialect_mir::ops::MirUndefOp::new(ctx, return_ty).get_operation();
266	                undef.deref_mut(ctx).set_loc(loc.clone());
267	                if let Some(prev) = prev_op {
268	                    undef.insert_after(ctx, prev);
269	                } else {
270	                    undef.insert_at_front(block_ptr, ctx);
271	                }
272	                (vec![undef.deref(ctx).get_result(0)], Some(undef))
273	            } else {
274	                (vec![], prev_op)
275	            }
276	        }
277	    };
278	
279	    let op = Operation::new(
280	        ctx,
281	        MirReturnOp::get_concrete_op_info(),
282	        vec![], // No results
283	        operands,
284	        vec![], // No successors
285	        0,      // No regions
286	    );
287	    op.deref_mut(ctx).set_loc(loc);
288	
289	    if let Some(prev) = terminator_prev_op {
290	        op.insert_after(ctx, prev);
291	    } else {
292	        op.insert_at_front(block_ptr, ctx);
293	    }
294	
295	    Ok(op)
296	}
297	
298	/// Translates a MIR `Goto` terminator to a zero-operand `mir.goto` operation.
299	///
300	/// Non-entry blocks carry no arguments; cross-block data flow travels through
301	/// per-local alloca slots instead.
302	#[allow(clippy::too_many_arguments)]
303	fn translate_goto(
304	    ctx: &mut Context,
305	    target: mir::BasicBlockIdx,
306	    block_ptr: Ptr<BasicBlock>,
307	    prev_op: Option<Ptr<Operation>>,
308	    block_map: &[Ptr<BasicBlock>],
309	    loc: Location,
310	) -> TranslationResult<Ptr<Operation>> {
311	    let target_idx: usize = target;
312	    let target_block = block_map[target_idx];
313	
314	    let op = Operation::new(
315	        ctx,
316	        MirGotoOp::get_concrete_op_info(),
317	        vec![],
318	        vec![],
319	        vec![target_block],
320	        0,
321	    );
322	    op.deref_mut(ctx).set_loc(loc);
323	
324	    if let Some(prev) = prev_op {
325	        op.insert_after(ctx, prev);
326	    } else {
327	        op.insert_at_front(block_ptr, ctx);
328	    }
329	
330	    Ok(op)
331	}
332	
333	/// Translates a MIR `Assert` terminator to a `mir.assert` operation.
334	///
335	/// Asserts that a condition matches the expected value, trapping on failure.
336	/// On success, branches to the target block.
337	///
338	/// # GPU Constraints
339	///
340	/// The CUDA toolchain does not support unwinding today; we treat all
341	/// unwind edges as unreachable.
342	///
343	/// # Condition Handling
344	///
345	/// If `expected == false`, the condition is negated before the assert:
346	/// - `assert!(cond, expected=true)` → assert condition is true
347	/// - `assert!(cond, expected=false)` → assert condition is false (negated)
348	#[allow(clippy::too_many_arguments)]
349	fn translate_assert(
350	    ctx: &mut Context,
351	    body: &mir::Body,
352	    cond: &mir::Operand,
353	    expected: bool,
354	    target: mir::BasicBlockIdx,
355	    unwind: &mir::UnwindAction,
356	    block_ptr: Ptr<BasicBlock>,
357	    prev_op: Option<Ptr<Operation>>,
358	    value_map: &mut ValueMap,
359	    block_map: &[Ptr<BasicBlock>],
360	    loc: Location,
361	) -> TranslationResult<Ptr<Operation>> {
362	    // The CUDA toolchain doesn't support stack unwinding today (the hardware
363	    // could, but nvcc/ptxas don't wire it up). We ignore the unwind action
364	    // and only generate code for the success path. External crates (like core)
365	    // may carry unwind edges in their MIR; those are dead code on GPU -- if a
366	    // panic occurs, the GPU thread traps.
367	    let _ = unwind;
368	
369	    // Translate the condition operand
370	    let (cond_value, mut last_inserted) = match cond {
371	        mir::Operand::Copy(place) | mir::Operand::Move(place) => {
372	            rvalue::translate_place(ctx, body, place, value_map, block_ptr, prev_op, loc.clone())?
373	        }
374	        _ => {
375	            return input_err!(
376	                loc.clone(),
377	                TranslationErr::unsupported(
378	                    "Constant conditions in assert not yet implemented".to_string(),
379	                )
380	            );
381	        }
382	    };
383	
384	    // Apply negation if expected == false
385	    let final_cond = if !expected {
386	        let bool_type = types::get_bool_type(ctx);
387	        let not_op = Operation::new(
388	            ctx,
389	            MirNotOp::get_concrete_op_info(),
390	            vec![bool_type.to_handle()],
391	            vec![cond_value],
392	            vec![],
393	            0,
394	        );
395	        not_op.deref_mut(ctx).set_loc(loc.clone());
396	
397	        if let Some(prev) = last_inserted {
398	            not_op.insert_after(ctx, prev);
399	        } else if let Some(prev) = prev_op {
400	            not_op.insert_after(ctx, prev);
401	        } else {
402	            not_op.insert_at_front(block_ptr, ctx);
403	        }
404	
405	        last_inserted = Some(not_op);
406	        not_op.deref(ctx).get_result(0)
407	    } else {
408	        cond_value
409	    };
410	
411	    // Alloca + load/store model: successor block has no arguments; assert
412	    // carries only its condition operand.
413	    let target_idx: usize = target;
414	    let target_block = block_map[target_idx];
415	
416	    let (flat_operands, segment_sizes) =
417	        MirAssertOp::compute_segment_sizes(vec![vec![final_cond], vec![]]);
418	
419	    let op = Operation::new(
420	        ctx,
421	        MirAssertOp::get_concrete_op_info(),
422	        vec![],
423	        flat_operands,
424	        vec![target_block],
425	        0,
426	    );
427	    Operation::get_op::<MirAssertOp>(op, ctx)
428	        .expect("MirAssertOp")
429	        .set_operand_segment_sizes(ctx, segment_sizes);
430	    op.deref_mut(ctx).set_loc(loc);
431	
432	    if let Some(prev) = last_inserted {
433	        op.insert_after(ctx, prev);
434	    } else if let Some(prev) = prev_op {
435	        op.insert_after(ctx, prev);
436	    } else {
437	        op.insert_at_front(block_ptr, ctx);
438	    }
439	
440	    Ok(op)
441	}
442	
443	/// Translates a MIR `SwitchInt` terminator to conditional branches.
444	///
445	/// Handles multi-way branches used for `match` expressions and enum dispatch:
446	///
447	/// # Boolean Switch (1 branch)
448	///
449	/// Uses `mir.cond_branch`:
450	/// - `switchInt(bool) → [0: bb_false, otherwise: bb_true]`
451	/// - Creates comparison or negation as needed
452	///
453	/// # Multi-way Switch (N branches)
454	///
455	/// Creates a chain of conditional branches:
456	/// ```text
457	/// current:     cmp0 = (discr == v0); cond_br cmp0, t0, intermediate_1
458	/// intermediate_1: cmp1 = (discr == v1); cond_br cmp1, t1, intermediate_2
459	/// ...
460	/// intermediate_N: cmpN = (discr == vN); cond_br cmpN, tN, otherwise
461	/// ```
462	#[allow(clippy::too_many_arguments)]
463	fn translate_switch(
464	    ctx: &mut Context,
465	    body: &mir::Body,
466	    discr: &mir::Operand,
467	    targets: &mir::SwitchTargets,
468	    block_ptr: Ptr<BasicBlock>,
469	    prev_op: Option<Ptr<Operation>>,
470	    value_map: &mut ValueMap,
471	    block_map: &[Ptr<BasicBlock>],
472	    loc: Location,
473	) -> TranslationResult<Ptr<Operation>> {
474	    use pliron::utils::apint::APInt;
475	    use std::num::NonZeroUsize;
476	
477	    // Translate discriminant
478	    let (discr_value, last_op) = match discr {
479	        mir::Operand::Copy(place) | mir::Operand::Move(place) => {
480	            rvalue::translate_place(ctx, body, place, value_map, block_ptr, prev_op, loc.clone())?
481	        }
482	        _ => {
483	            rvalue::translate_operand(ctx, body, discr, value_map, block_ptr, prev_op, loc.clone())?
484	        }
485	    };
486	
487	    let branches: Vec<_> = targets.branches().collect();
488	    let otherwise_idx: usize = targets.otherwise();
489	
490	    // For bool switches (2 branches), use MirCondBranchOp
491	    if branches.len() == 1 {
492	        let (val, target_bb) = branches[0];
493	        let target_idx: usize = target_bb;
494	
495	        // For MirCondBranchOp, we need an i1 (boolean) condition.
496	        // If discr is already i1, we can use it directly (with appropriate target ordering).
497	        // Otherwise, we need to create a comparison: (discr == val).
498	        use pliron::r#type::Typed;
499	        let discr_ty = discr_value.get_type(ctx);
500	        let bool_ty = types::get_bool_type(ctx);
501	
502	        let (condition, last_inserted_op) = if discr_ty == bool_ty.to_handle() {
503	            // discr is already i1
504	            // For boolean switch: val=0 means "if false", val=1 means "if true"
505	            // switchInt(bool) -> [0: bb_false, otherwise: bb_true]
506	            // Since val == 0 means "go to target when discr == 0", we need condition = !discr
507	            if val == 0 {
508	                // Create NOT operation: condition = !discr
509	                let not_op = Operation::new(
510	                    ctx,
511	                    MirNotOp::get_concrete_op_info(),
512	                    vec![bool_ty.to_handle()],
513	                    vec![discr_value],
514	                    vec![],
515	                    0,
516	                );
517	                not_op.deref_mut(ctx).set_loc(loc.clone());
518	                if let Some(prev) = last_op {
519	                    not_op.insert_after(ctx, prev);
520	                } else {
521	                    not_op.insert_at_front(block_ptr, ctx);
522	                }
523	                let cond = not_op.deref(ctx).get_result(0);
524	                (cond, Some(not_op))
525	            } else {
526	                // val == 1: condition is discr itself
527	                (discr_value, last_op)
528	            }
529	        } else {
530	            // discr is not i1 (e.g., u32 from lane_id(), or enum discriminant)
531	            // Create comparison: condition = (discr == val)
532	            let (width, signedness) =
533	                if let Some(int_ty) = discr_ty.deref(ctx).downcast_ref::<IntegerType>() {
534	                    (int_ty.width() as usize, int_ty.signedness())
535	                } else {
536	                    (64, Signedness::Unsigned) // Default to 64-bit unsigned if we can't determine
537	                };
538	
539	            // Create constant for val with SAME type as discriminant.
540	            // SwitchInt values are u128 bit patterns at the discriminant's
541	            // width; the dialect stores tags as u64 (same limit as
542	            // MirEnumType::variant_discriminants in types.rs), so values
543	            // that need more than 64 bits must fail loudly instead of
544	            // silently truncating.
545	            let switch_val = u64::try_from(val).map_err(|_| {
546	                input_error!(
547	                    loc.clone(),
548	                    TranslationErr::unsupported(format!(
549	                        "SwitchInt value {} does not fit in 64 bits",
550	                        val
551	                    ))
552	                )
553	            })?;
554	            let width_nz = NonZeroUsize::new(width).unwrap();
555	            let apint = APInt::from_u64(switch_val, width_nz);
556	            let int_attr = pliron::builtin::attributes::IntegerAttr::new(
557	                IntegerType::get(ctx, width as u32, signedness),
558	                apint,
559	            );
560	
561	            let const_op = Operation::new(
562	                ctx,
563	                MirConstantOp::get_concrete_op_info(),
564	                vec![discr_ty],
565	                vec![],
566	                vec![],
567	                0,
568	            );
569	            const_op.deref_mut(ctx).set_loc(loc.clone());
570	            let const_op_wrapped = MirConstantOp::new(const_op);
571	            const_op_wrapped.set_attr_value(ctx, int_attr);
572	
573	            if let Some(prev) = last_op {
574	                const_op_wrapped.get_operation().insert_after(ctx, prev);
575	            } else {
576	                const_op_wrapped
577	                    .get_operation()
578	                    .insert_at_front(block_ptr, ctx);
579	            }
580	            let const_val = const_op_wrapped.get_operation().deref(ctx).get_result(0);
581	
582	            // Create comparison operation: discr == val
583	            let eq_op = Operation::new(
584	                ctx,
585	                MirEqOp::get_concrete_op_info(),
586	                vec![bool_ty.to_handle()],
587	                vec![discr_value, const_val],
588	                vec![],
589	                0,
590	            );
591	            eq_op.deref_mut(ctx).set_loc(loc.clone());
592	            eq_op.insert_after(ctx, const_op_wrapped.get_operation());
593	
594	            let cond = eq_op.deref(ctx).get_result(0);
595	            (cond, Some(eq_op))
596	        };
597	
598	        // With condition = (discr == val) [or !discr for boolean val==0 case]:
599	        // true_target = target (go here when condition is true, i.e., discr == val)
600	        // false_target = otherwise (go here when condition is false)
601	        let true_idx = target_idx;
602	        let false_idx = otherwise_idx;
603	
604	        let true_block = block_map[true_idx];
605	        let false_block = block_map[false_idx];
606	
607	        // Alloca + load/store model: both branch successors are argument-less;
608	        // the cond_br carries only its boolean condition.
609	        let (flat_operands, segment_sizes) =
610	            MirCondBranchOp::compute_segment_sizes(vec![vec![condition], vec![], vec![]]);
611	
612	        let op = Operation::new(
613	            ctx,
614	            MirCondBranchOp::get_concrete_op_info(),
615	            vec![],
616	            flat_operands,
617	            vec![true_block, false_block],
618	            0,
619	        );
620	        Operation::get_op::<MirCondBranchOp>(op, ctx)
621	            .expect("MirCondBranchOp")
622	            .set_operand_segment_sizes(ctx, segment_sizes);
623	        op.deref_mut(ctx).set_loc(loc);
624	
625	        // Use last_inserted_op (which accounts for NOT/EQ ops created above)
626	        if let Some(prev) = last_inserted_op {
627	            op.insert_after(ctx, prev);
628	        } else if let Some(prev) = last_op {
629	            op.insert_after(ctx, prev);
630	        } else {
631	            op.insert_at_front(block_ptr, ctx);
632	        }
633	
634	        return Ok(op);
635	    }
636	
637	    // For multi-way switches, create a chain of conditional branches
638	    // switchInt(discr) -> [v0: t0, v1: t1, ..., otherwise: default]
639	    // Becomes:
640	    //   current_block: cmp0 = discr == v0; cond_br cmp0, t0, intermediate_1
641	    //   intermediate_1: cmp1 = discr == v1; cond_br cmp1, t1, intermediate_2
642	    //   ...
643	    //   intermediate_N-1: cmpN = discr == v(N-1); cond_br cmpN, t(N-1), default
644	    use pliron::r#type::Typed;
645	
646	    let n = branches.len();
647	    let discr_ty = discr_value.get_type(ctx);
648	    let bool_ty = types::get_bool_type(ctx);
649	    let (width, signedness) =
650	        if let Some(int_ty) = discr_ty.deref(ctx).downcast_ref::<IntegerType>() {
651	            (int_ty.width() as usize, int_ty.signedness())
652	        } else {
653	            (64, Signedness::Unsigned) // Default to 64-bit unsigned
654	        };
655	
656	    // Create N-1 intermediate blocks for the comparison chain
657	    let mut intermediate_blocks: Vec<Ptr<BasicBlock>> = Vec::new();
658	    let mut prev_block = block_ptr;
659	    for _ in 0..(n - 1) {
660	        let intermediate = BasicBlock::new(ctx, None, vec![]);
661	        intermediate.insert_after(ctx, prev_block);
662	        intermediate_blocks.push(intermediate);
663	        prev_block = intermediate;
664	    }
665	
666	    // Process each branch in the chain
667	    let mut current_block_ptr = block_ptr;
668	    let mut current_prev_op = last_op;
669	
670	    for (i, (val, target_bb)) in branches.iter().enumerate() {
671	        let target_idx: usize = *target_bb;
672	        let target_block = block_map[target_idx];
673	
674	        // Determine the "else" block (next in chain or otherwise).
675	        let else_block: Ptr<BasicBlock> = if i < n - 1 {
676	            intermediate_blocks[i]
677	        } else {
678	            block_map[otherwise_idx]
679	        };
680	
681	        // Create constant for comparison with SAME type as discriminant.
682	        // Same checked u128 -> u64 narrowing as the single-branch path
683	        // above: a silently truncated switch value would compare against
684	        // the wrong arm.
685	        let switch_val = u64::try_from(*val).map_err(|_| {
686	            input_error!(
687	                loc.clone(),
688	                TranslationErr::unsupported(format!(
689	                    "SwitchInt value {} does not fit in 64 bits",
690	                    val
691	                ))
692	            )
693	        })?;
694	        let width_nz = NonZeroUsize::new(width).unwrap();
695	        let apint = APInt::from_u64(switch_val, width_nz);
696	        let int_attr = pliron::builtin::attributes::IntegerAttr::new(
697	            IntegerType::get(ctx, width as u32, signedness),
698	            apint,
699	        );
700	
701	        let const_op = Operation::new(
702	            ctx,
703	            MirConstantOp::get_concrete_op_info(),
704	            vec![discr_ty],
705	            vec![],
706	            vec![],
707	            0,
708	        );
709	        const_op.deref_mut(ctx).set_loc(loc.clone());
710	        let const_op_wrapped = MirConstantOp::new(const_op);
711	        const_op_wrapped.set_attr_value(ctx, int_attr);
712	
713	        if let Some(prev) = current_prev_op {
714	            const_op_wrapped.get_operation().insert_after(ctx, prev);
715	        } else {
716	            const_op_wrapped
717	                .get_operation()
718	                .insert_at_front(current_block_ptr, ctx);
719	        }
720	
721	        let const_val = const_op_wrapped.get_operation().deref(ctx).get_result(0);
722	
723	        // Create comparison: discr == val
724	        let cmp_op = Operation::new(
725	            ctx,
726	            MirEqOp::get_concrete_op_info(),
727	            vec![bool_ty.to_handle()],
728	            vec![discr_value, const_val],
729	            vec![],
730	            0,
731	        );
732	        cmp_op.deref_mut(ctx).set_loc(loc.clone());
733	        cmp_op.insert_after(ctx, const_op_wrapped.get_operation());
734	
735	        let condition = cmp_op.deref(ctx).get_result(0);
736	
737	        // Alloca + load/store model: argument-less successor blocks; the
738	        // cond_br carries only its boolean condition.
739	        let (flat_operands, segment_sizes) =
740	            MirCondBranchOp::compute_segment_sizes(vec![vec![condition], vec![], vec![]]);
741	
742	        let branch_op = Operation::new(
743	            ctx,
744	            MirCondBranchOp::get_concrete_op_info(),
745	            vec![],
746	            flat_operands,
747	            vec![target_block, else_block],
748	            0,
749	        );
750	        Operation::get_op::<MirCondBranchOp>(branch_op, ctx)
751	            .expect("MirCondBranchOp")
752	            .set_operand_segment_sizes(ctx, segment_sizes);
753	        branch_op.deref_mut(ctx).set_loc(loc.clone());
754	        branch_op.insert_after(ctx, cmp_op);
755	
756	        // Move to next intermediate block for next iteration
757	        if i < n - 1 {
758	            current_block_ptr = intermediate_blocks[i];
759	            current_prev_op = None;
760	        }
761	    }
762	
763	    // Return the terminator from the original block
764	    let first_branch = block_ptr
765	        .deref(ctx)
766	        .iter(ctx)
767	        .last()
768	        .expect("Block should have terminator after multi-way switch translation");
769	
770	    Ok(first_branch)
771	}
772	
773	/// Translates a MIR `Drop` terminator.
774	///
775	/// rustc emits `TerminatorKind::Drop` only for places whose type has drop
776	/// glue. cuda-oxide does not yet emit device-side `drop_in_place` calls,
777	/// so a destructor that actually does something cannot run on the device.
778	///
779	/// Two cases:
780	///
781	/// 1. **Provably no-op glue**: when the monomorphized drop glue does
782	///    nothing observable (checked by [`drop_glue::drop_glue_is_noop`]),
783	///    the terminator lowers to a plain branch to its target block.
784	///    The common source pattern is `for x in arr` over a by-value
785	///    array: the loop's `core::array::IntoIter<T, N>` has an
786	///    `impl Drop`, but for element types without drop glue that
787	///    destructor folds to nothing.
788	///
789	/// 2. **Genuinely effectful glue**: we surface a hard error with the
790	///    dropped place's type so the user can diagnose and restructure
791	///    the kernel. Lowering to a goto here would silently skip the
792	///    destructor and miscompile.
793	///
794	/// Suppressing drop glue on a Copy-shaped value (e.g. wrapping in
795	/// `core::mem::ManuallyDrop`) prevents the Drop terminator from being
796	/// emitted in the first place and lets the kernel compile.
797	///
798	/// The unwind action is ignored: device code is panic=abort, and for the
799	/// no-op case there is nothing that could unwind anyway.
800	#[allow(clippy::too_many_arguments)]
801	fn translate_drop(
802	    ctx: &mut Context,
803	    body: &mir::Body,
804	    place: &mir::Place,
805	    target: mir::BasicBlockIdx,
806	    _unwind: &mir::UnwindAction,
807	    block_ptr: Ptr<BasicBlock>,
808	    prev_op: Option<Ptr<Operation>>,
809	    block_map: &[Ptr<BasicBlock>],
810	    loc: Location,
811	) -> TranslationResult<Ptr<Operation>> {
812	    let dropped_ty = place.ty(body.locals()).map_err(|e| {
813	        input_error!(
814	            loc.clone(),
815	            TranslationErr::unsupported(format!(
816	                "drop terminator: failed to compute place type: {e:?}"
817	            ))
818	        )
819	    })?;
820	    if drop_glue::drop_glue_is_noop(dropped_ty) {
821	        return translate_goto(ctx, target, block_ptr, prev_op, block_map, loc);
822	    }
823	    input_err!(
824	        loc,
825	        TranslationErr::unsupported(format!(
826	            "drop of `{:?}` is not supported on the device; its destructor \
827	             does observable work and cuda-oxide does not yet emit \
828	             device-side `drop_in_place` calls. Restructure the kernel to \
829	             use only `Copy` types, or wrap the value in \
830	             `core::mem::ManuallyDrop` to suppress drop glue.",
831	            dropped_ty.kind()
832	        ))
833	    )
834	}
835	
836	// ============================================================================
837	// Call Translation (includes intrinsic dispatch)
838	// ============================================================================
839	
840	/// Translates a MIR `Call` terminator to Pliron IR operations.
841	///
842	/// This is the main entry point for function call translation. It handles:
843	///
844	/// 1. **Intrinsic dispatch**: Calls to `cuda_device::*` are expanded inline
845	///    to `dialect-nvvm` operations (thread IDs, barriers, warp ops, etc.)
846	///
847	/// 2. **Closure calls**: `FnOnce::call_once`, `FnMut::call_mut`, `Fn::call`
848	///    require unpacking tuple arguments before calling the closure body
849	///
850	/// 3. **Regular calls**: Other functions are emitted as `mir.call` operations
851	///
852	/// # GPU Constraints
853	///
854	/// Unwind edges are treated as unreachable (CUDA toolchain limitation, not HW).
855	///
856	/// # Flow
857	///
858	/// ```text
859	/// Call → extract_func_info → try_dispatch_intrinsic
860	///                         ↓ (if intrinsic)
861	///                         intrinsics::* handlers
862	///                         ↓ (if closure)
863	///                         translate_closure_call
864	///                         ↓ (otherwise)
865	///                         helpers::emit_function_call
866	/// ```
867	#[allow(clippy::too_many_arguments)]
868	fn translate_call(
869	    ctx: &mut Context,
870	    body: &mir::Body,
871	    func: &mir::Operand,
872	    args: &[mir::Operand],
873	    destination: &mir::Place,
874	    target: &Option<mir::BasicBlockIdx>,
875	    unwind: &mir::UnwindAction,
876	    block_ptr: Ptr<BasicBlock>,
877	    prev_op: Option<Ptr<Operation>>,
878	    value_map: &mut ValueMap,
879	    block_map: &[Ptr<BasicBlock>],
880	    loc: Location,
881	    legaliser: &mut Legaliser,
882	) -> TranslationResult<Ptr<Operation>> {
883	    // See comment in translate_assert for rationale.
884	    let _ = unwind;
885	
886	    // Convert target to Option<usize>
887	    let target_usize = target.map(|t| t);
888	
889	    // Extract function info
890	    let (pattern_name, call_name, substs_str) = extract_func_info(func);
891	
892	    // Helper to check if substitutions contain a type
893	    let substs_contains =
894	        |pattern: &str| -> bool { substs_str.as_ref().is_some_and(|s| s.contains(pattern)) };
895	
896	    // Skip precondition_check calls - these are UB check assertions that are
897	    // dead code because we return false for RuntimeChecks(UbChecks).
898	    // The MIR still contains these calls, but they're in dead branches.
899	    if let Some(ref name) = pattern_name
900	        && name.contains("precondition_check")
901	    {
902	        // Just emit a goto to the target block, skipping the call entirely
903	        if let Some(target_idx) = target_usize {
904	            let actual_prev_op = if let Some(p) = prev_op {
905	                p
906	            } else {
907	                // Create a dummy i1 constant (false) as a placeholder operation
908	                use pliron::builtin::attributes::IntegerAttr;
909	                use pliron::utils::apint::APInt;
910	                use std::num::NonZeroUsize;
911	
912	                let bool_ty = IntegerType::get(ctx, 1, Signedness::Signless);
913	                let dummy = Operation::new(
914	                    ctx,
915	                    MirConstantOp::get_concrete_op_info(),
916	                    vec![bool_ty.into()],
917	                    vec![],
918	                    vec![],
919	                    0,
920	                );
921	                dummy.deref_mut(ctx).set_loc(loc.clone());
922	                let const_op = MirConstantOp::new(dummy);
923	                let false_val = APInt::from_u64(0, NonZeroUsize::new(1).unwrap());
924	                const_op.set_attr_value(ctx, IntegerAttr::new(bool_ty, false_val));
925	                let dummy = const_op.get_operation();
926	                dummy.insert_at_front(block_ptr, ctx);
927	                dummy
928	            };
929	            return Ok(helpers::emit_goto(
930	                ctx,
931	                target_idx,
932	                actual_prev_op,
933	                block_map,
934	                loc,
935	            ));
936	        }
937	    }
938	
939	    // Identify the actual core callable-trait methods. Matching text in an
940	    // arbitrary function name is not sufficient: a user function named, for
941	    // example, `call_once_helper` must remain an ordinary call.
942	    if let Some(call_info) = callable_trait_call_info(func)
943	        && !args.is_empty()
944	    {
945	        if receiver_is_closure(&args[0], body) {
946	            return translate_closure_call(
947	                ctx,
948	                body,
949	                &call_name,
950	                call_info.resolved_is_shim,
951	                args,
952	                destination,
953	                &target_usize,
954	                block_ptr,
955	                prev_op,
956	                value_map,
957	                block_map,
958	                loc,
959	                legaliser,
960	            );
961	        }
962	        if let Some(function_item) = extract_function_item_target(&args[0], body) {
963	            return translate_function_item_call(
964	                ctx,
965	                body,
966	                &function_item,
967	                args,
968	                destination,
969	                &target_usize,
970	                block_ptr,
971	                prev_op,
972	                value_map,
973	                block_map,
974	                loc,
975	                legaliser,
976	            );
977	        }
978	    }
979	
980	    // Handle prof_trigger specially to extract const generic N
981	    if let Some(ref name) = pattern_name
982	        && name == "cuda_device::debug::prof_trigger"
983	    {
984	        // Extract the const generic N from the function type
985	        if let mir::Operand::Constant(const_op) = func
986	            && let rustc_public::ty::TyKind::RigidTy(rustc_public::ty::RigidTy::FnDef(_, substs)) =
987	                const_op.const_.ty().kind()
988	        {
989	            // The const generic N is the first generic argument
990	            if let Some(rustc_public::ty::GenericArgKind::Const(c)) = substs.0.first() {
991	                use rustc_public::ty::TyConstKind;
992	
993	                let event_id = match c.kind() {
994	                    TyConstKind::Value(_, alloc) => {
995	                        // Read the allocation bytes (little-endian u32)
996	                        alloc.read_uint().unwrap_or(0) as u32
997	                    }
998	                    _ => {
999	                        // Try eval_target_usize as fallback
1000	                        c.eval_target_usize().unwrap_or(0) as u32
1001	                    }
1002	                };
1003	                return intrinsics::debug::emit_prof_trigger(
1004	                    ctx,
1005	                    event_id,
1006	                    &target_usize,
1007	                    block_ptr,
1008	                    prev_op,
1009	                    block_map,
1010	                    loc,
1011	                );
1012	            }
1013	        }
1014	    }
1015	
1016	    // Per-loop unroll marker from `#[unroll]` / `#[unroll(N)]`. The enclosing
1017	    // `#[kernel]` or `#[device]` macro injects this call at the start of the loop
1018	    // body, so we plant a `mir.unroll_hint` op right here, inside that loop
1019	    // body. The loop-unroll pass later maps the hint back to its enclosing loop
1020	    // (via LoopInfo) and consumes it, so it never reaches lowering. The factor
1021	    // is the call's const generic (`0` = full unroll). Then branch to the
1022	    // target as usual.
1023	    // Match both the full path (`cuda_device::thread::__unroll_config`) and the
1024	    // re-exported short path (`cuda_device::__unroll_config`), mirroring the
1025	    // robust suffix match in `body::detect_unroll_config`.
1026	    if let Some(ref name) = pattern_name
1027	        && (name == "__unroll_config" || name.ends_with("::__unroll_config"))
1028	    {
1029	        let Some(factor) = extract_unroll_factor(func) else {
1030	            return input_err!(
1031	                loc,
1032	                TranslationErr::invalid_op(
1033	                    "could not read the const-generic factor from an unroll marker",
1034	                )
1035	            );
1036	        };
1037	        let Some(target) = target_usize else {
1038	            return input_err!(
1039	                loc,
1040	                TranslationErr::invalid_op("an unroll marker call has no return target")
1041	            );
1042	        };
1043	        let hint = MirUnrollHintOp::new(ctx, factor).get_operation();
1044	        hint.deref_mut(ctx).set_loc(loc.clone());
1045	        match prev_op {
1046	            Some(prev) => hint.insert_after(ctx, prev),
1047	            None => hint.insert_at_front(block_ptr, ctx),
1048	        }
1049	        return Ok(helpers::emit_goto(ctx, target, hint, block_map, loc));
1050	    }
1051	
1052	    // Handle DynamicSharedArray specially to extract the ALIGN const generic
1053	    if let Some(ref name) = pattern_name
1054	        && name.contains("DynamicSharedArray")
1055	        && (name.contains("::get") || name.contains("::offset"))
1056	    {
1057	        // Extract the ALIGN const generic from the function type
1058	        // DynamicSharedArray<T, ALIGN> has T as first generic, ALIGN as second
1059	        let alignment = if let mir::Operand::Constant(const_op) = func {
1060	            if let rustc_public::ty::TyKind::RigidTy(rustc_public::ty::RigidTy::FnDef(_, substs)) =
1061	                const_op.const_.ty().kind()
1062	            {
1063	                // The ALIGN const generic is the second generic argument (index 1)
1064	                // First is T (type), second is ALIGN (const)
1065	                if let Some(rustc_public::ty::GenericArgKind::Const(c)) = substs.0.get(1) {
1066	                    use rustc_public::ty::TyConstKind;
1067	                    match c.kind() {
1068	                        TyConstKind::Value(_, alloc) => alloc.read_uint().unwrap_or(16) as u64,
1069	                        _ => c.eval_target_usize().unwrap_or(16),
1070	                    }
1071	                } else {
1072	                    16 // Default alignment (matches nvcc)
1073	                }
1074	            } else {
1075	                16
1076	            }
1077	        } else {
1078	            16
1079	        };
1080	
1081	        if name.contains("::get") {
1082	            // Both get() and get_raw() use the same handler with offset 0
1083	            return intrinsics::memory::emit_dynamic_shared_get(
1084	                ctx,
1085	                body,
1086	                args,
1087	                destination,
1088	                &target_usize,
1089	                block_ptr,
1090	                prev_op,
1091	                value_map,
1092	                block_map,
1093	                loc,
1094	                0,         // byte_offset = 0 for get() and get_raw()
1095	                alignment, // User-specified or default alignment
1096	            );
1097	        } else if name.contains("::offset") {
1098	            // DynamicSharedArray::offset(byte_offset) - get pointer at byte offset
1099	            return intrinsics::memory::emit_dynamic_shared_offset(
1100	                ctx,
1101	                body,
1102	                args,
1103	                destination,
1104	                &target_usize,
1105	                block_ptr,
1106	                prev_op,
1107	                value_map,
1108	                block_map,
1109	                loc,
1110	                alignment, // User-specified or default alignment
1111	            );
1112	        }
1113	    }
1114	
1115	    // Try to dispatch core::sync::atomic intrinsics (std::intrinsics::atomic_*)
1116	    // These use const generics for ordering, so we intercept them here before
1117	    // the regular intrinsic dispatch and extract generics from the func operand.
1118	    if let Some(ref name) = pattern_name
1119	        && intrinsics::atomic::is_core_atomic_intrinsic(name)
1120	    {
1121	        return intrinsics::atomic::dispatch_core_intrinsic(
1122	            ctx,
1123	            body,
1124	            func,
1125	            args,
1126	            destination,
1127	            &target_usize,
1128	            block_ptr,
1129	            prev_op,
1130	            value_map,
1131	            block_map,
1132	            loc,
1133	            name,
1134	        );
1135	    }
1136	
1137	    // `assert_inhabited::<T>()` is a compile-time validity check that rustc
1138	    // plants in `MaybeUninit::assume_init_read`, which the `for x in arr`
1139	    // loop machinery calls for every yielded element (issue #138). The
1140	    // intrinsic panics only when `T` has no possible values at all (an
1141	    // "uninhabited" type such as `core::convert::Infallible`); for any
1142	    // ordinary type it compiles to nothing. We decide which case applies
1143	    // from the monomorphized type's layout: uninhabited types are exactly
1144	    // those whose layout has `VariantsShape::Empty`. Inhabited types lower
1145	    // to a unit no-op; uninhabited ones lower to `unreachable`, matching
1146	    // how device code models panics (they cannot execute on the GPU).
1147	    // If the generic argument or its layout cannot be read, fall through
1148	    // to the loud "not yet supported" rejection below.
1149	    if let Some(ref name) = pattern_name
1150	        && (name == "core::intrinsics::assert_inhabited"
1151	            || name == "std::intrinsics::assert_inhabited")
1152	        && let mir::Operand::Constant(const_op) = func
1153	        && let rustc_public::ty::TyKind::RigidTy(rustc_public::ty::RigidTy::FnDef(_, substs)) =
1154	            const_op.const_.ty().kind()
1155	        && let Some(rustc_public::ty::GenericArgKind::Type(checked_ty)) = substs.0.first()
1156	        && let Ok(layout) = checked_ty.layout()
1157	    {
1158	        let uninhabited = matches!(
1159	            layout.shape().variants,
1160	            rustc_public::abi::VariantsShape::Empty
1161	        );
1162	        if uninhabited {
1163	            let op = Operation::new(
1164	                ctx,
1165	                dialect_mir::ops::MirUnreachableOp::get_concrete_op_info(),
1166	                vec![],
1167	                vec![],
1168	                vec![],
1169	                0,
1170	            );
1171	            op.deref_mut(ctx).set_loc(loc);
1172	            if let Some(prev) = prev_op {
1173	                op.insert_after(ctx, prev);
1174	            } else {
1175	                op.insert_at_front(block_ptr, ctx);
1176	            }
1177	            return Ok(op);
1178	        }
1179	        return helpers::emit_unit_noop_intrinsic(
1180	            ctx,
1181	            destination,
1182	            &target_usize,
1183	            block_ptr,
1184	            prev_op,
1185	            value_map,
1186	            block_map,
1187	            loc,
1188	            name,
1189	        );
1190	    }
1191	
1192	    // Try to dispatch as intrinsic
1193	    if let Some(ref name) = pattern_name
1194	        && let Some(result) = try_dispatch_intrinsic(
1195	            ctx,
1196	            body,
1197	            name,
1198	            args,
1199	            destination,
1200	            &target_usize,
1201	            block_ptr,
1202	            prev_op,
1203	            value_map,
1204	            block_map,
1205	            loc.clone(),
1206	            &substs_contains,
1207	        )?
1208	    {
1209	        return Ok(result);
1210	    }
1211	
1212	    // Handle diverging calls (calls that never return, like unwrap_failed, panic, etc.)
1213	    // These have no target block because the function never returns.
1214	    // In GPU code, we emit an unreachable terminator since panics can't actually execute.
1215	    if target_usize.is_none() {
1216	        // This is a diverging call (returns !) - emit unreachable
1217	        // Examples: unwrap_failed(), panic!(), abort()
1218	        return Ok(emit_unreachable_after(ctx, block_ptr, prev_op, loc));
1219	    }
1220	
1221	    // A call to a rustc intrinsic that no dispatch arm above recognized can
1222	    // never be emitted as a regular function call: rustc resolves intrinsics
1223	    // to `InstanceKind::Intrinsic`, the collector skips those by design, so
1224	    // no definition for the symbol will ever exist in the module. Emitting
1225	    // the call anyway would only fail much later, as a confusing
1226	    // "Symbol ... not found" verifier error on the LLVM dialect module.
1227	    // Fail here instead, with the intrinsic's name and source location, so
1228	    // each gap surfaces as an actionable per-site diagnostic (issue #137).
1229	    if let Some(ref name) = pattern_name
1230	        && (name.starts_with("core::intrinsics::") || name.starts_with("std::intrinsics::"))
1231	    {
1232	        return input_err!(
1233	            loc,
1234	            TranslationErr::unsupported(format!(
1235	                "rustc intrinsic `{name}` is not yet supported on the device"
1236	            ))
1237	        );
1238	    }
1239	
1240	    // The collector skips the entire `libm` crate: every libm call must be
1241	    // intercepted by the float-math dispatch above and rerouted to a
1242	    // libdevice intrinsic, so no definition for a libm symbol ever exists in
1243	    // the module. A libm function the dispatch does not recognize would only
1244	    // fail much later, as a bare "Symbol libm__cbrtf not found" verifier
1245	    // error on the LLVM dialect module. Fail here instead, with the
1246	    // function's name and source location.
1247	    if let Some(ref name) = pattern_name
1248	        && intrinsics::float_math::is_libm_path(name)
1249	    {
1250	        return input_err!(
1251	            loc,
1252	            TranslationErr::unsupported(format!(
1253	                "libm function `{name}` is not yet mapped to a libdevice intrinsic; \
1254	                 add it to `from_libm_path` in the mir-importer float-math dispatch"
1255	            ))
1256	        );
1257	    }
1258	
1259	    // Not an intrinsic - emit regular function call
1260	    let raw_name = call_name.unwrap_or_else(|| "unknown_function".to_string());
1261	    let legal_name = legaliser.legalise(&raw_name);
1262	
1263	    // Type the call result from the caller's destination place, not from the
1264	    // callee's declared signature. The declared signature of a trait method
1265	    // is written against the trait, so its return type can be an unresolved
1266	    // associated-type projection such as `<&Foo as Mul>::Output` (issue #133),
1267	    // which the type translator cannot turn into a concrete layout. The
1268	    // destination local in the caller's monomorphized MIR already has that
1269	    // projection resolved (`Foo`), and it is by construction the exact type
1270	    // the call result is stored into, so the `mir.call` result type and the
1271	    // destination slot always agree. The callee `mir.func` return type is
1272	    // independently derived from the callee body's return place, which is
1273	    // normalized the same way, so caller and callee stay consistent.
1274	    let return_type = types::translate_destination_type(ctx, body, destination, &loc)?;
1275	
1276	    helpers::emit_function_call(
1277	        ctx,
1278	        body,
1279	        &legal_name,
1280	        args,
1281	        destination,
1282	        return_type,
1283	        &target_usize,
1284	        block_ptr,
1285	        prev_op,
1286	        value_map,
1287	        block_map,
1288	        loc,
1289	    )
1290	}
1291	
1292	/// Handle `FnOnce::call_once`, `FnMut::call_mut`, or `Fn::call` when the
1293	/// receiver is a function item.
1294	///
1295	/// Rust-call ABI passes `(self, tuple_args)` to the trait shim, but the
1296	/// function item's real body expects only the tuple elements as ordinary
1297	/// arguments. Lowering the shim as an ordinary call leaves a dangling
1298	/// `<fn item as FnOnce>::call_once` symbol because no MIR body is collected
1299	/// for that shim.
1300	#[allow(clippy::too_many_arguments)]
1301	fn translate_function_item_call(
1302	    ctx: &mut Context,
1303	    body: &mir::Body,
1304	    function_item: &FunctionItemTarget,
1305	    args: &[mir::Operand],
1306	    destination: &mir::Place,
1307	    target: &Option<usize>,
1308	    block_ptr: Ptr<BasicBlock>,
1309	    prev_op: Option<Ptr<Operation>>,
1310	    value_map: &mut ValueMap,
1311	    block_map: &[Ptr<BasicBlock>],
1312	    loc: Location,
1313	    legaliser: &mut Legaliser,
1314	) -> TranslationResult<Ptr<Operation>> {
1315	    use dialect_mir::ops::{MirCallOp, MirExtractFieldOp};
1316	    use pliron::builtin::attributes::StringAttr;
1317	    use pliron::identifier::Identifier;
1318	
1319	    if function_item.requires_direct_dispatch {
1320	        return input_err!(
1321	            loc,
1322	            TranslationErr::unsupported(format!(
1323	                "calling `{}` through Fn/FnMut/FnOnce is not yet supported because that target requires intrinsic or external dispatch; wrap it in a local `#[device]` function and pass the wrapper instead",
1324	                function_item.name
1325	            ))
1326	        );
1327	    }
1328	
1329	    let return_type = types::translate_destination_type(ctx, body, destination, &loc)?;
1330	    let callee = legaliser.legalise(&function_item.name).to_string();
1331	
1332	    let mut unpacked_args = Vec::new();
1333	    let mut last_op = prev_op;
1334	
1335	    if let Some(tuple_arg) = args.get(1) {
1336	        let (tuple_value, tuple_last_op) = rvalue::translate_operand(
1337	            ctx,
1338	            body,
1339	            tuple_arg,
1340	            value_map,
1341	            block_ptr,
1342	            last_op,
1343	            loc.clone(),
1344	        )?;
1345	        last_op = tuple_last_op;
1346	
1347	        let tuple_ty = tuple_value.get_type(ctx);
1348	        let element_types: Option<Vec<_>> = {
1349	            let tuple_ty_obj = tuple_ty.deref(ctx);
1350	            tuple_ty_obj
1351	                .downcast_ref::<dialect_mir::types::MirTupleType>()
1352	                .map(|mir_tuple_ty| mir_tuple_ty.get_types().to_vec())
1353	        };
1354	
1355	        if let Some(element_types) = element_types {
1356	            for (i, elem_ty) in element_types.iter().enumerate() {
1357	                let extract_op = Operation::new(
1358	                    ctx,
1359	                    MirExtractFieldOp::get_concrete_op_info(),
1360	                    vec![*elem_ty],
1361	                    vec![tuple_value],
1362	                    vec![],
1363	                    0,
1364	                );
1365	                extract_op.deref_mut(ctx).set_loc(loc.clone());
1366	
1367	                let mir_extract = MirExtractFieldOp::new(extract_op);
1368	                mir_extract.set_attr_index(ctx, dialect_mir::attributes::FieldIndexAttr(i as u32));
1369	
1370	                if let Some(prev) = last_op {
1371	                    extract_op.insert_after(ctx, prev);
1372	                } else {
1373	                    extract_op.insert_at_front(block_ptr, ctx);
1374	                }
1375	                last_op = Some(extract_op);
1376	                unpacked_args.push(extract_op.deref(ctx).get_result(0));
1377	            }
1378	        } else {
1379	            unpacked_args.push(tuple_value);
1380	        }
1381	    }
1382	
1383	    let call_op = Operation::new(
1384	        ctx,
1385	        MirCallOp::get_concrete_op_info(),
1386	        vec![return_type],
1387	        unpacked_args,
1388	        vec![],
1389	        0,
1390	    );
1391	    call_op.deref_mut(ctx).set_loc(loc.clone());
1392	
1393	    call_op.deref_mut(ctx).attributes.set(
1394	        Identifier::try_from("callee").unwrap(),
1395	        StringAttr::new(callee),
1396	    );
1397	
1398	    if let Some(prev) = last_op {
1399	        call_op.insert_after(ctx, prev);
1400	    } else {
1401	        call_op.insert_at_front(block_ptr, ctx);
1402	    }
1403	
1404	    if target.is_none() {
1405	        return Ok(emit_unreachable_after(ctx, block_ptr, Some(call_op), loc));
1406	    }
1407	
1408	    let result_value = call_op.deref(ctx).get_result(0);
1409	    let last_inserted = value_map
1410	        .store_local(
1411	            ctx,
1412	            destination.local,
1413	            result_value,
1414	            block_ptr,
1415	            Some(call_op),
1416	        )
1417	        .unwrap_or(call_op);
1418	
1419	    if let Some(target_idx) = target {
1420	        Ok(helpers::emit_goto(
1421	            ctx,
1422	            *target_idx,
1423	            last_inserted,
1424	            block_map,
1425	            loc,
1426	        ))
1427	    } else {
1428	        Ok(call_op)
1429	    }
1430	}
1431	
1432	/// Handle closure trait method calls (FnOnce::call_once, FnMut::call_mut, Fn::call).
1433	///
1434	/// These calls pass arguments as a tuple, but the closure body expects unpacked args:
1435	/// - MIR: `<{closure} as FnMut<(u32,)>>::call_mut(self_ref, tuple_args)`
1436	/// - Closure body expects: `fn(self_ref, unpacked_arg1, unpacked_arg2, ...)`
1437	///
1438	/// We detect these calls and unpack the tuple argument before calling the closure.
1439	///
1440	/// ## Important: Closure Body Resolution
1441	///
1442	/// In unified compilation with `std`, `Instance::resolve` for `<Closure as FnOnce>::call_once`
1443	/// returns a trait method **shim**, not the closure body directly. We must extract the closure's
1444	/// DefId from `args[0]`'s type and resolve that to get the actual closure body's mangled name.
1445	///
1446	/// See `device_closures/README.md` for detailed documentation.
1447	#[allow(clippy::too_many_arguments)]
1448	fn translate_closure_call(
1449	    ctx: &mut Context,
1450	    body: &mir::Body,
1451	    call_name: &Option<String>,
1452	    resolved_is_shim: bool,
1453	    args: &[mir::Operand],
1454	    destination: &mir::Place,
1455	    target: &Option<usize>,
1456	    block_ptr: Ptr<BasicBlock>,
1457	    prev_op: Option<Ptr<Operation>>,
1458	    value_map: &mut ValueMap,
1459	    block_map: &[Ptr<BasicBlock>],
1460	    loc: Location,
1461	    legaliser: &mut Legaliser,
1462	) -> TranslationResult<Ptr<Operation>> {
1463	    use dialect_mir::ops::{MirCallOp, MirExtractFieldOp};
1464	    use pliron::builtin::attributes::{IntegerAttr, StringAttr};
1465	    use pliron::identifier::Identifier;
1466	    use pliron::r#type::Typed;
1467	    use pliron::utils::apint::APInt;
1468	    use std::num::NonZeroUsize;
1469	
1470	    // Same reasoning as the regular-call path: the trait-level signature of
1471	    // `FnOnce::call_once` types its result as the projection
1472	    // `<{closure} as FnOnce<Args>>::Output`. The caller's destination local
1473	    // carries the already-resolved concrete type, so use that.
1474	    let return_type = types::translate_destination_type(ctx, body, destination, &loc)?;
1475	
1476	    // Extract the closure body's name from the closure type in args[0].
1477	    // This is critical for unified compilation where Instance::resolve returns
1478	    // a trait shim instead of the closure body directly.
1479	    let closure_body_name = extract_closure_body_name(&args[0], body);
1480	
1481	    let raw_callee = closure_body_name
1482	        .or_else(|| call_name.as_ref().map(|s| s.to_string()))
1483	        .unwrap_or_else(|| "unknown_closure".to_string());
1484	    let callee = legaliser.legalise(&raw_callee).to_string();
1485	
1486	    // Translate self argument (args[0])
1487	    let (self_value, mut last_op) = rvalue::translate_operand(
1488	        ctx,
1489	        body,
1490	        &args[0],
1491	        value_map,
1492	        block_ptr,
1493	        prev_op,
1494	        loc.clone(),
1495	    )?;
1496	
1497	    // Translate tuple argument (args[1])
1498	    let (tuple_value, tuple_last_op) = rvalue::translate_operand(
1499	        ctx,
1500	        body,
1501	        &args[1],
1502	        value_map,
1503	        block_ptr,
1504	        last_op,
1505	        loc.clone(),
1506	    )?;
1507	    last_op = tuple_last_op;
1508	
1509	    // Determine whether the resolved closure body expects a reference.
1510	    //
1511	    // In `std` mode, rustc generates `FnOnce::call_once(self, args)` which passes
1512	    // the closure BY VALUE. But the closure body expects `&self` (a reference).
1513	    //
1514	    // In `no_std` mode, rustc generates `FnMut::call_mut(&mut self, args)` which
1515	    // already passes a reference.
1516	    //
1517	    // When we have call_once, we need to create a reference to the closure value
1518	    // before calling the closure body.
1519	    //
1520	    // A compiler shim for `FnOnce` over an `Fn`/`FnMut` closure receives the
1521	    // closure by value but calls a body that expects a reference. A genuine
1522	    // by-value `FnOnce` closure resolves directly to its body (`Item`) and must
1523	    // stay by value. Calls whose MIR receiver is already a reference need no
1524	    // extra borrow in either case.
1525	    let receiver_needs_borrow = resolved_is_shim
1526	        && operand_type(&args[0], body).is_some_and(|ty| {
1527	            !matches!(
1528	                ty.kind(),
1529	                rustc_public::ty::TyKind::RigidTy(rustc_public::ty::RigidTy::Ref(_, _, _))
1530	            )
1531	        });
1532	
1533	    let self_arg = if receiver_needs_borrow {
1534	        // For call_once: self is passed by value, but closure body expects reference.
1535	        // Create a MirRefOp to take a reference to the closure value.
1536	        let self_ty = self_value.get_type(ctx);
1537	        let ptr_ty = dialect_mir::types::MirPtrType::get(ctx, self_ty, true, 0);
1538	
1539	        let ref_op = Operation::new(
1540	            ctx,
1541	            dialect_mir::ops::MirRefOp::get_concrete_op_info(),
1542	            vec![ptr_ty.into()],
1543	            vec![self_value],
1544	            vec![],
1545	            0,
1546	        );
1547	        ref_op.deref_mut(ctx).set_loc(loc.clone());
1548	
1549	        // Set mutable attribute (true for &mut)
1550	        let bool_type = IntegerType::get(ctx, 1, Signedness::Unsigned);
1551	        let mutable_attr =
1552	            IntegerAttr::new(bool_type, APInt::from_i64(1, NonZeroUsize::new(1).unwrap()));
1553	        ref_op
1554	            .deref_mut(ctx)
1555	            .attributes
1556	            .set(Identifier::try_from("mutable").unwrap(), mutable_attr);
1557	
1558	        // Insert after previous op
1559	        if let Some(prev) = last_op {
1560	            ref_op.insert_after(ctx, prev);
1561	        } else {
1562	            ref_op.insert_at_front(block_ptr, ctx);
1563	        }
1564	        last_op = Some(ref_op);
1565	
1566	        // Use the reference as self arg
1567	        ref_op.deref(ctx).get_result(0)
1568	    } else {
1569	        // For call_mut/call: self is already a reference, use as-is
1570	        self_value
1571	    };
1572	
1573	    // Build unpacked arguments: start with self (or ref to self for call_once)
1574	    let mut unpacked_args = vec![self_arg];
1575	
1576	    // Unpack the tuple - extract each field
1577	    let tuple_ty = tuple_value.get_type(ctx);
1578	    let element_types: Option<Vec<_>> = {
1579	        let tuple_ty_obj = tuple_ty.deref(ctx);
1580	        tuple_ty_obj
1581	            .downcast_ref::<dialect_mir::types::MirTupleType>()
1582	            .map(|mir_tuple_ty| mir_tuple_ty.get_types().to_vec())
1583	    };
1584	
1585	    if let Some(element_types) = element_types {
1586	        for (i, elem_ty) in element_types.iter().enumerate() {
1587	            // Create extract_field operation
1588	            let extract_op = Operation::new(
1589	                ctx,
1590	                MirExtractFieldOp::get_concrete_op_info(),
1591	                vec![*elem_ty],
1592	                vec![tuple_value],
1593	                vec![],
1594	                0,
1595	            );
1596	            extract_op.deref_mut(ctx).set_loc(loc.clone());
1597	
1598	            let mir_extract = MirExtractFieldOp::new(extract_op);
1599	            mir_extract.set_attr_index(ctx, dialect_mir::attributes::FieldIndexAttr(i as u32));
1600	
1601	            // Insert after previous op
1602	            if let Some(prev) = last_op {
1603	                extract_op.insert_after(ctx, prev);
1604	            } else {
1605	                extract_op.insert_at_front(block_ptr, ctx);
1606	            }
1607	            last_op = Some(extract_op);
1608	
1609	            // Get the extracted value
1610	            let elem_value = extract_op.deref(ctx).get_result(0);
1611	            unpacked_args.push(elem_value);
1612	        }
1613	    } else {
1614	        // Not a tuple type - just pass as is (single arg case)
1615	        unpacked_args.push(tuple_value);
1616	    }
1617	
1618	    // Now emit the call with unpacked arguments
1619	    let call_op = Operation::new(
1620	        ctx,
1621	        MirCallOp::get_concrete_op_info(),
1622	        vec![return_type],
1623	        unpacked_args,
1624	        vec![],
1625	        0,
1626	    );
1627	    call_op.deref_mut(ctx).set_loc(loc.clone());
1628	
1629	    // Set callee attribute
1630	    let callee_attr = StringAttr::new(callee);
1631	    call_op
1632	        .deref_mut(ctx)
1633	        .attributes
1634	        .set(Identifier::try_from("callee").unwrap(), callee_attr);
1635	
1636	    // Insert the call
1637	    let call_op = if let Some(prev) = last_op {
1638	        call_op.insert_after(ctx, prev);
1639	        call_op
1640	    } else {
1641	        call_op.insert_at_front(block_ptr, ctx);
1642	        call_op
1643	    };
1644	
1645	    if target.is_none() {
1646	        return Ok(emit_unreachable_after(ctx, block_ptr, Some(call_op), loc));
1647	    }
1648	
1649	    // Store the call result into the destination local's slot.
1650	    let result_value = call_op.deref(ctx).get_result(0);
1651	    let last_inserted = value_map
1652	        .store_local(
1653	            ctx,
1654	            destination.local,
1655	            result_value,
1656	            block_ptr,
1657	            Some(call_op),
1658	        )
1659	        .unwrap_or(call_op);
1660	
1661	    // Emit goto to target
1662	    if let Some(target_idx) = target {
1663	        Ok(helpers::emit_goto(
1664	            ctx,
1665	            *target_idx,
1666	            last_inserted,
1667	            block_map,
1668	            loc,
1669	        ))
1670	    } else {
1671	        Ok(call_op)
1672	    }
1673	}
1674	
1675	fn emit_unreachable_after(
1676	    ctx: &mut Context,
1677	    block_ptr: Ptr<BasicBlock>,
1678	    prev_op: Option<Ptr<Operation>>,
1679	    loc: Location,
1680	) -> Ptr<Operation> {
1681	    let op = Operation::new(
1682	        ctx,
1683	        dialect_mir::ops::MirUnreachableOp::get_concrete_op_info(),
1684	        vec![],
1685	        vec![],
1686	        vec![],
1687	        0,
1688	    );
1689	    op.deref_mut(ctx).set_loc(loc);
1690	    if let Some(prev) = prev_op {
1691	        op.insert_after(ctx, prev);
1692	    } else {
1693	        op.insert_at_front(block_ptr, ctx);
1694	    }
1695	    op
1696	}

1698	/// True only when the rust-call receiver is itself a closure.
1699	fn receiver_is_closure(receiver: &mir::Operand, body: &mir::Body) -> bool {
1700	    let Some(ty) = operand_type(receiver, body) else {
1701	        return false;
1702	    };
1703	
1704	    let inner = match ty.kind() {
1705	        rustc_public::ty::TyKind::RigidTy(rustc_public::ty::RigidTy::Ref(_, inner, _)) => inner,
1706	        _ => ty,
1707	    };
1708	
1709	    matches!(
1710	        inner.kind(),
1711	        rustc_public::ty::TyKind::RigidTy(rustc_public::ty::RigidTy::Closure(_, _))
1712	    )
1713	}

1715	struct FunctionItemTarget {
1716	    name: String,
1717	    requires_direct_dispatch: bool,
1718	}
1719	
1720	fn extract_function_item_target(
1721	    receiver: &mir::Operand,
1722	    body: &mir::Body,
1723	) -> Option<FunctionItemTarget> {
1724	    let ty = operand_type(receiver, body)?;
1725	    let inner = match ty.kind() {
1726	        rustc_public::ty::TyKind::RigidTy(rustc_public::ty::RigidTy::Ref(_, inner, _)) => inner,
1727	        _ => ty,
1728	    };
1729	
1730	    let rustc_public::ty::TyKind::RigidTy(rustc_public::ty::RigidTy::FnDef(fn_def, substs)) =
1731	        inner.kind()
1732	    else {
1733	        return None;
1734	    };
1735	
1736	    let instance = rustc_public::mir::mono::Instance::resolve(fn_def, &substs).ok()?;
1737	    let crate_name = fn_def.krate().name;
1738	    Some(FunctionItemTarget {
1739	        name: function_item_call_name(instance),
1740	        requires_direct_dispatch: fn_def.is_intrinsic()
1741	            || instance.is_foreign_item()
1742	            || !instance.has_body()
1743	            || matches!(crate_name.as_str(), "cuda_device" | "cuda-device" | "libm"),
1744	    })
1745	}

1747	fn function_item_call_name(instance: rustc_public::mir::mono::Instance) -> String {
1748	    if instance.is_foreign_item() || !instance.args().0.is_empty() {
1749	        instance.mangled_name()
1750	    } else {
1751	        instance.name().to_string()
1752	    }
1753	}

1755	fn operand_type(receiver: &mir::Operand, body: &mir::Body) -> Option<rustc_public::ty::Ty> {
1756	    receiver.ty(body.locals()).ok()
1757	}

1759	struct CallableTraitCallInfo {
1760	    resolved_is_shim: bool,
1761	}
1762	
1763	/// Recognize the three callable traits by compiler identity available through
1764	/// `rustc_public`.
1765	///
1766	/// These methods are defined in `core`, use the rust-call ABI, and have an
1767	/// exact parent/method pair (`Fn::call`, `FnMut::call_mut`, or
1768	/// `FnOnce::call_once`). The combined check cannot be spoofed by a user item
1769	/// whose name merely contains one of those strings. The resolved instance may
1770	/// be either a shim or the closure body itself, so instance kind is returned as
1771	/// lowering information rather than used as the recognition predicate.
1772	fn callable_trait_call_info(func: &mir::Operand) -> Option<CallableTraitCallInfo> {
1773	    use rustc_public::mir::mono::{Instance, InstanceKind};
1774	    use rustc_public::ty::{Abi, RigidTy, TyKind};
1775	
1776	    let mir::Operand::Constant(const_op) = func else {
1777	        return None;
1778	    };
1779	    let TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::FnDef(fn_def, substs)) = const_op.const_.ty().kind() else {
1780	        return None;
1781	    };
1782	    if fn_def.fn_sig().skip_binder().abi != Abi::RustCall || fn_def.krate().name.as_str() != "core"
1783	    {
1784	        return None;
1785	    }
1786	
1787	    let method_name = fn_def.def_id().name();
1788	    let method = method_name.as_str().rsplit("::").next()?;
1789	    let parent_name = fn_def.def_id().parent()?.name();
1790	    let parent = parent_name.as_str().rsplit("::").next()?;
1791	    let is_callable_method = matches!(
1792	        (parent, method),
1793	        ("Fn", "call") | ("FnMut", "call_mut") | ("FnOnce", "call_once")
1794	    );
1795	    if !is_callable_method {
1796	        return None;
1797	    }
1798	
1799	    let instance = Instance::resolve(fn_def, &substs).ok()?;
1800	    Some(CallableTraitCallInfo {
1801	        resolved_is_shim: instance.kind == InstanceKind::Shim,
1802	    })
1803	}
1804	
1805	/// Extracts the closure body's mangled name from a closure operand.
1806	///
1807	/// In unified compilation with `std`, when we see a call like:
1808	///   `<{closure} as FnOnce<(u32,)>>::call_once(closure_ref, args_tuple)`
1809	///
1810	/// The `call_name` from `Instance::resolve` gives us the trait method shim's name,
1811	/// not the closure body. We need to extract the closure's DefId from `args[0]`'s type
1812	/// and resolve that to get the actual closure body's mangled name.
1813	///
1814	/// The closure argument can be:
1815	/// - A direct closure value (type is `Closure(def, substs)`)
1816	/// - A reference to a closure (type is `Ref(_, Closure(def, substs), _)`)
1817	/// - A mutable reference (same pattern)
1818	fn extract_closure_body_name(closure_arg: &mir::Operand, body: &mir::Body) -> Option<String> {
1819	    let closure_ty = operand_type(closure_arg, body)?;
1820	
1821	    // Unwrap references to get the actual closure type
1822	    let inner_ty = match closure_ty.kind() {
1823	        rustc_public::ty::TyKind::RigidTy(rustc_public::ty::RigidTy::Ref(_, inner, _)) => inner,
1824	        _ => closure_ty,
1825	    };
1826	
1827	    // Extract the closure DefId and substs
1828	    let (closure_def, substs) = match inner_ty.kind() {
1829	        rustc_public::ty::TyKind::RigidTy(rustc_public::ty::RigidTy::Closure(def, substs)) => {
1830	            (def, substs.clone())
1831	        }
1832	        _ => return None,
1833	    };
1834	
1835	    // Get the closure body instance directly, NOT through resolve_closure
1836	    // (which returns a call_once shim in unified/std compilation).
1837	    //
1838	    // The closure_def.def_id() gives us the DefId of the closure body.
1839	    // We construct the mangled name by creating an FnDef and resolving it.
1840	    use rustc_public::mir::mono::Instance;
1841	    use rustc_public::ty::FnDef;
1842	
1843	    // Create an FnDef from the closure's DefId
1844	    let fn_def = FnDef(closure_def.def_id());
1845	
1846	    if let Ok(instance) = Instance::resolve(fn_def, &substs) {
1847	        return Some(instance.mangled_name());
1848	    }
1849	
1850	    // Fallback: try the old resolve_closure method
1851	    Instance::resolve_closure(closure_def, &substs, rustc_public::ty::ClosureKind::FnOnce)
1852	        .ok()
1853	        .map(|instance| instance.mangled_name())
1854	}
1855	
1856	/// Read the const-generic `FACTOR` from a `__unroll_config::<FACTOR>()` callee
1857	/// (`0` = full unroll).
1858	///
1859	/// Returns `None` when the callee is malformed instead of silently turning the
1860	/// request into a full unroll.
1861	fn extract_unroll_factor(func: &mir::Operand) -> Option<u32> {
1862	    use rustc_public::ty::{RigidTy, TyConstKind, TyKind};
1863	    let mir::Operand::Constant(constant) = func else {
1864	        return None;
1865	    };
1866	    let TyKind::RigidTy(RigidTy::FnDef(_, args)) = constant.const_.ty().kind() else {
1867	        return None;
1868	    };
1869	    if let Some(arg) = args.0.first()
1870	        && let rustc_public::ty::GenericArgKind::Const(c) = arg
1871	    {
1872	        return match c.kind() {
1873	            TyConstKind::Value(_, alloc) => {
1874	                alloc.read_uint().ok().and_then(|v| u32::try_from(v).ok())
1875	            }
1876	            _ => c
1877	                .eval_target_usize()
1878	                .ok()
1879	                .and_then(|v| u32::try_from(v).ok()),
1880	        };
1881	    }
1882	    None
1883	}

1885	/// Extracts function metadata from a MIR function operand.
1886	///
1887	/// Returns a tuple of:
1888	/// - `pattern_name`: The function's simple name (e.g., `"cuda_device::index_1d"`)
1889	/// - `call_name`: The name used for the call target in generated code
1890	/// - `substs_str`: Debug string of generic substitutions (for pattern matching)
1891	///
1892	/// Deliberately NOT returned: the callee's declared return type. The
1893	/// declared `fn_sig` of a trait method is written against the trait, so its
1894	/// output can be an unresolved associated-type projection such as
1895	/// `<&Foo as Mul>::Output` (issue #133). Call results are instead typed from
1896	/// the caller's destination place, which rustc has already monomorphized and
1897	/// normalized. If a callee-signature type is ever genuinely needed here,
1898	/// resolve the instance first (`Instance::resolve`) and query the signature
1899	/// on the resolved instance so associated types arrive normalized.
1900	///
1901	/// This information is used to:
1902	/// 1. Match intrinsic patterns by `pattern_name` (full FQDN, e.g. `cuda_device::thread::threadIdx_x`)
1903	/// 2. Check for closure types via `substs_str.contains("Closure")`
1904	/// 3. Generate the correct call target name (FQDN for non-generic, mangled for generic)
1905	///
1906	/// # Naming strategy
1907	///
1908	/// `CrateDef::name()` returns the fully qualified name (FQDN) in the
1909	/// `rustc_public` API (e.g. `helper_fn::cuda_oxide_device_<hash>_vecadd_device`).
1910	/// We use the raw `FnDef` name as `pattern_name` for intrinsic matching, then
1911	/// use the resolved `Instance::name()` as `call_name` for monomorphic calls.
1912	/// Raw `FnDef` substitutions are not authoritative for this decision: concrete
1913	/// trait impl calls can carry a trait self type in the operand while resolving
1914	/// to a monomorphic instance, and the resolved impl FQDN can differ from the
1915	/// trait item FQDN.
1916	///
1917	/// For resolved instances that still carry generic args, `Instance::mangled_name`
1918	/// is used instead, which the collector also matches via `compute_export_name`.
1919	/// Non-generic FQDNs with characters such as `<`, `>`, and `::` are passed raw to
1920	/// the same pliron `Legaliser` used for definition symbols.
1921	///
1922	/// Foreign items (`extern "C"` block declarations) are the exception: they
1923	/// have no MIR body, so the collector never exports a definition under the
1924	/// FQDN and the device linker (libdevice, external LTOIR) only knows the
1925	/// link symbol. `call_name` for those is `Instance::mangled_name`, which is
1926	/// the link symbol (it honours `#[link_name]`).
1927	fn extract_func_info(func: &mir::Operand) -> (Option<String>, Option<String>, Option<String>) {
1928	    match func {
1929	        mir::Operand::Constant(const_op) => match const_op.const_.kind() {
1930	            ConstantKind::ZeroSized => {
1931	                let ty_kind = const_op.const_.ty().kind();
1932	                match &ty_kind {
1933	                    rustc_public::ty::TyKind::RigidTy(rustc_public::ty::RigidTy::FnDef(
1934	                        fn_def,
1935	                        substs,
1936	                    )) => {
1937	                        use rustc_public::mir::mono::Instance;
1938	
1939	                        let pattern_name = fn_def.name().as_str().to_string();
1940	
1941	                        let resolved = Instance::resolve(*fn_def, substs).ok();
1942	                        let call_name = if let Some(instance) = resolved {
1943	                            if instance.is_foreign_item() {
1944	                                // Foreign items (`extern "C"` blocks) have no MIR
1945	                                // body, so no definition is ever exported under
1946	                                // the FQDN. Emit the call under the link symbol
1947	                                // (e.g. `__nv_asinf`), which is what libdevice or
1948	                                // externally linked LTOIR actually provides.
1949	                                instance.mangled_name()
1950	                            } else if !instance.args().0.is_empty() {
1951	                                instance.mangled_name()
1952	                            } else {
1953	                                instance.name().to_string()
1954	                            }
1955	                        } else {
1956	                            pattern_name.clone()
1957	                        };
1958	
1959	                        let substs_debug = format!("{:?}", substs);
1960	                        (Some(pattern_name), Some(call_name), Some(substs_debug))
1961	                    }
1962	                    _ => (None, None, None),
1963	                }
1964	            }
1965	            _ => (None, None, None),
1966	        },
1967	        _ => (None, None, None),
1968	    }
1969	}
