1971	/// Lower `core::intrinsics::typed_swap_nonoverlapping::<T>(x, y)`, the
1972	/// primitive behind `core::mem::swap`/`mem::replace`, as load/load/store/store.
1973	/// The two pointers are guaranteed non-overlapping, so the temp-free crossover
1974	/// `t0 = *x; t1 = *y; *x = t1; *y = t0` is valid (the loaded SSA values are
1975	/// captured before either store runs). Returns a unit result + goto target.
1976	#[allow(clippy::too_many_arguments)]
1977	fn emit_typed_swap(
1978	    ctx: &mut Context,
1979	    body: &mir::Body,
1980	    args: &[mir::Operand],
1981	    destination: &mir::Place,
1982	    target: &Option<usize>,
1983	    block_ptr: Ptr<BasicBlock>,
1984	    prev_op: Option<Ptr<Operation>>,
1985	    value_map: &mut ValueMap,
1986	    block_map: &[Ptr<BasicBlock>],
1987	    loc: Location,
1988	) -> TranslationResult<Ptr<Operation>> {
1989	    use dialect_mir::ops::{MirConstructTupleOp, MirLoadOp, MirStoreOp};
1990	    use dialect_mir::types::{MirPtrType, MirTupleType};
1991	
1992	    if args.len() != 2 {
1993	        return input_err!(
1994	            loc,
1995	            TranslationErr::unsupported(
1996	                "typed_swap_nonoverlapping requires two pointer operands".to_string()
1997	            )
1998	        );
1999	    }
2000	
2001	    let (ptr_x, last) = rvalue::translate_operand(
2002	        ctx,
2003	        body,
2004	        &args[0],
2005	        value_map,
2006	        block_ptr,
2007	        prev_op,
2008	        loc.clone(),
2009	    )?;
2010	    let (ptr_y, last) =
2011	        rvalue::translate_operand(ctx, body, &args[1], value_map, block_ptr, last, loc.clone())?;
2012	
2013	    let elem_ty = {
2014	        let t = ptr_x.get_type(ctx);
2015	        let r = t.deref(ctx);
2016	        match r.downcast_ref::<MirPtrType>() {
2017	            Some(p) => p.pointee,
2018	            None => {
2019	                return input_err!(
2020	                    loc,
2021	                    TranslationErr::unsupported(
2022	                        "typed_swap_nonoverlapping operand is not a pointer".to_string()
2023	                    )
2024	                );
2025	            }
2026	        }
2027	    };
2028	
2029	    // t0 = *x
2030	    let load_x = Operation::new(
2031	        ctx,
2032	        MirLoadOp::get_concrete_op_info(),
2033	        vec![elem_ty],
2034	        vec![ptr_x],
2035	        vec![],
2036	        0,
2037	    );
2038	    load_x.deref_mut(ctx).set_loc(loc.clone());
2039	    match last {
2040	        Some(p) => load_x.insert_after(ctx, p),
2041	        None => load_x.insert_at_front(block_ptr, ctx),
2042	    }
2043	    let vx = load_x.deref(ctx).get_result(0);
2044	
2045	    // t1 = *y
2046	    let load_y = Operation::new(
2047	        ctx,
2048	        MirLoadOp::get_concrete_op_info(),
2049	        vec![elem_ty],
2050	        vec![ptr_y],
2051	        vec![],
2052	        0,
2053	    );
2054	    load_y.deref_mut(ctx).set_loc(loc.clone());
2055	    load_y.insert_after(ctx, load_x);
2056	    let vy = load_y.deref(ctx).get_result(0);
2057	
2058	    // *x = t1
2059	    let store_x = Operation::new(
2060	        ctx,
2061	        MirStoreOp::get_concrete_op_info(),
2062	        vec![],
2063	        vec![ptr_x, vy],
2064	        vec![],
2065	        0,
2066	    );
2067	    store_x.deref_mut(ctx).set_loc(loc.clone());
2068	    store_x.insert_after(ctx, load_y);
2069	
2070	    // *y = t0
2071	    let store_y = Operation::new(
2072	        ctx,
2073	        MirStoreOp::get_concrete_op_info(),
2074	        vec![],
2075	        vec![ptr_y, vx],
2076	        vec![],
2077	        0,
2078	    );
2079	    store_y.deref_mut(ctx).set_loc(loc.clone());
2080	    store_y.insert_after(ctx, store_x);
2081	
2082	    // unit result
2083	    let unit_ty = MirTupleType::get(ctx, vec![]);
2084	    let unit_op = Operation::new(
2085	        ctx,
2086	        MirConstructTupleOp::get_concrete_op_info(),
2087	        vec![unit_ty.into()],
2088	        vec![],
2089	        vec![],
2090	        0,
2091	    );
2092	    unit_op.deref_mut(ctx).set_loc(loc.clone());
2093	    unit_op.insert_after(ctx, store_y);
2094	    let unit_val = unit_op.deref(ctx).get_result(0);
2095	
2096	    let goto_prev = value_map
2097	        .store_local(ctx, destination.local, unit_val, block_ptr, Some(unit_op))
2098	        .unwrap_or(unit_op);
2099	
2100	    if let Some(target_idx) = target {
2101	        Ok(helpers::emit_goto(
2102	            ctx,
2103	            *target_idx,
2104	            goto_prev,
2105	            block_map,
2106	            loc,
2107	        ))
2108	    } else {
2109	        input_err!(
2110	            loc,
2111	            TranslationErr::unsupported(
2112	                "typed_swap_nonoverlapping call without target not supported".to_string()
2113	            )
2114	        )
2115	    }
2116	}

2118	/// Dispatches `cuda_device` intrinsic calls to their respective handlers.
2119	///
2120	/// Returns `Ok(Some(op))` if the call was an intrinsic, `Ok(None)` otherwise.
2121	///
2122	/// # Intrinsic Categories
2123	///
2124	/// | Category          | Examples                                          |
2125	/// |-------------------|---------------------------------------------------|
2126	/// | Thread Position   | `threadIdx_x`, `blockIdx_y`, `blockDim_x`         |
2127	/// | Index Helpers     | `index_1d`, `index_2d::<S>`, `index_2d_runtime`, `index_2d_row`, `index_2d_col` |
2128	/// | Synchronization   | `sync_threads`, `mbarrier_*`, `fence_*`           |
2129	/// | Warp Primitives   | `shuffle_*`, `vote_*`, `lane_id`                  |
2130	/// | WGMMA (Hopper)    | `wgmma_fence`, `wgmma_mma_*`, `make_smem_desc`    |
2131	/// | TMA               | `cp_async_bulk_tensor_*_g2s/s2g`, `wait_group`    |
2132	/// | Tcgen05 (Blackwell)| `tcgen05_alloc`, `tcgen05_mma_*`, `tcgen05_ld_*` |
2133	/// | Memory            | `SharedArray::index`, `stmatrix_*`, `cvt_*`       |
2134	/// | DisjointSlice     | `get_thread_local`, `len`                         |
2135	#[allow(clippy::too_many_arguments)]
2136	fn try_dispatch_intrinsic(
2137	    ctx: &mut Context,
2138	    body: &mir::Body,
2139	    name: &str,
2140	    args: &[mir::Operand],
2141	    destination: &mir::Place,
2142	    target: &Option<usize>,
2143	    block_ptr: Ptr<BasicBlock>,
2144	    prev_op: Option<Ptr<Operation>>,
2145	    value_map: &mut ValueMap,
2146	    block_map: &[Ptr<BasicBlock>],
2147	    loc: Location,
2148	    substs_contains: &impl Fn(&str) -> bool,
2149	) -> TranslationResult<Option<Ptr<Operation>>> {
2150	    if let Some(kind) = intrinsics::asm::InlinePtxCallKind::from_path(name) {
2151	        return Ok(Some(intrinsics::asm::emit_inline_ptx(
2152	            ctx,
2153	            body,
2154	            args,
2155	            destination,
2156	            target,
2157	            block_ptr,
2158	            prev_op,
2159	            value_map,
2160	            block_map,
2161	            loc,
2162	            kind,
2163	        )?));
2164	    }
2165	
2166	    if name == "core::intrinsics::typed_swap_nonoverlapping"
2167	        || name == "std::intrinsics::typed_swap_nonoverlapping"
2168	    {
2169	        return Ok(Some(emit_typed_swap(
2170	            ctx,
2171	            body,
2172	            args,
2173	            destination,
2174	            target,
2175	            block_ptr,
2176	            prev_op,
2177	            value_map,
2178	            block_map,
2179	            loc,
2180	        )?));
2181	    }
2182	
2183	    if let Some(intrinsic) = intrinsics::bitops::RustBitIntrinsic::from_core_path(name) {
2184	        return Ok(Some(intrinsics::bitops::emit_rust_bit_intrinsic(
2185	            ctx,
2186	            body,
2187	            intrinsic,
2188	            args,
2189	            destination,
2190	            target,
2191	            block_ptr,
2192	            prev_op,
2193	            value_map,
2194	            block_map,
2195	            loc,
2196	        )?));
2197	    }
2198	
2199	    if let Some(intrinsic) = intrinsics::saturating::RustSaturatingIntrinsic::from_core_path(name) {
2200	        return Ok(Some(
2201	            intrinsics::saturating::emit_rust_saturating_intrinsic(
2202	                ctx,
2203	                body,
2204	                intrinsic,
2205	                args,
2206	                destination,
2207	                target,
2208	                block_ptr,
2209	                prev_op,
2210	                value_map,
2211	                block_map,
2212	                loc,
2213	            )?,
2214	        ));
2215	    }
2216	
2217	    if let Some(intrinsic) = intrinsics::bigint::RustBigIntIntrinsic::from_core_path(name) {
2218	        return Ok(Some(intrinsics::bigint::emit_rust_bigint_intrinsic(
2219	            ctx,
2220	            body,
2221	            intrinsic,
2222	            args,
2223	            destination,
2224	            target,
2225	            block_ptr,
2226	            prev_op,
2227	            value_map,
2228	            block_map,
2229	            loc,
2230	        )?));
2231	    }
2232

6	//! Warp-level matrix intrinsics (`movmatrix`, `mma.sync`).
7	
8	use super::super::helpers::emit_store_result_and_goto;
9	use crate::error::{TranslationErr, TranslationResult};
10	use crate::translator::rvalue;
11	use crate::translator::values::ValueMap;
12	use dialect_mir::{
13	    attributes::FieldIndexAttr,
14	    ops::{MirConstructArrayOp, MirExtractFieldOp},
15	    types::MirArrayType,
16	};
17	use dialect_nvvm::ops::{
18	    MmaM8N8K4F64Op, MmaM16N8K8F32Tf32Op, MmaM16N8K16F32Bf16Op, MmaM16N8K16F32F16Op,
19	    MmaM16N8K32S32S8Op, MovmatrixTransB16Op,
20	};
21	use pliron::basic_block::BasicBlock;
22	use pliron::builtin::types::{FP32Type, FP64Type, IntegerType, Signedness};
23	use pliron::context::{Context, Ptr};
24	use pliron::input_err;
25	use pliron::location::{Located, Location};
26	use pliron::op::Op;
27	use pliron::operation::Operation;
28	use pliron::r#type::{TypeHandle, Typed};
29	use pliron::value::Value;
30	use rustc_public::mir;
31	
32	/// Emit movmatrix_trans_b16: in-register 8×8 matrix transpose.
33	///
34	/// Takes one u32 operand and returns one u32.
35	#[allow(clippy::too_many_arguments)]
36	pub fn emit_movmatrix_trans_b16(
37	    ctx: &mut Context,
38	    body: &mir::Body,
39	    args: &[mir::Operand],
40	    destination: &mir::Place,
41	    target: &Option<usize>,
42	    block_ptr: Ptr<BasicBlock>,
43	    prev_op: Option<Ptr<Operation>>,
44	    value_map: &mut ValueMap,
45	    block_map: &[Ptr<BasicBlock>],
46	    loc: Location,
47	) -> TranslationResult<Ptr<Operation>> {
48	    if args.len() != 1 {
49	        return input_err!(
50	            loc.clone(),
51	            TranslationErr::unsupported(format!(
52	                "movmatrix_trans_b16 expects 1 argument, got {}",
53	                args.len()
54	            ))
55	        );
56	    }
57	
58	    let (a_val, last_op) = rvalue::translate_operand(
59	        ctx,
60	        body,
61	        &args[0],
62	        value_map,
63	        block_ptr,
64	        prev_op,
65	        loc.clone(),
66	    )?;
67	
68	    let u32_ty = IntegerType::get(ctx, 32, Signedness::Unsigned);
69	
70	    let mov_op = Operation::new(
71	        ctx,
72	        MovmatrixTransB16Op::get_concrete_op_info(),
73	        vec![u32_ty.into()],
74	        vec![a_val],
75	        vec![],
76	        0,
77	    );
78	    mov_op.deref_mut(ctx).set_loc(loc.clone());
79	
80	    if let Some(prev) = last_op {
81	        mov_op.insert_after(ctx, prev);
82	    } else {
83	        mov_op.insert_at_front(block_ptr, ctx);
84	    }
85	
86	    let result = mov_op.deref(ctx).get_result(0);
87	    emit_store_result_and_goto(
88	        ctx,
89	        destination,
90	        result,
91	        target,
92	        block_ptr,
93	        mov_op,
94	        value_map,
95	        block_map,
96	        loc,
97	        "movmatrix_trans_b16 call without target block",
98	    )
99	}
100	
101	/// Extract a fixed-size Rust array into scalar SSA register values.
102	///
103	/// Constant-field extraction lowers to LLVM `extractvalue`, so no temporary
104	/// stack slot is introduced for the MMA fragments.
105	fn extract_array_registers(
106	    ctx: &mut Context,
107	    array: Value,
108	    expected_element_ty: TypeHandle,
109	    expected_len: usize,
110	    block_ptr: Ptr<BasicBlock>,
111	    mut last_op: Option<Ptr<Operation>>,
112	    loc: Location,
113	    fragment_name: &str,
114	) -> TranslationResult<(Vec<Value>, Ptr<Operation>)> {
115	    let array_ty = array.get_type(ctx);
116	    let valid_array = {
117	        let array_ty = array_ty.deref(ctx);
118	        array_ty
119	            .downcast_ref::<MirArrayType>()
120	            .is_some_and(|array_ty| {
121	                array_ty.size() == expected_len as u64
122	                    && array_ty.element_type() == expected_element_ty
123	            })
124	    };
125	    if !valid_array {
126	        return input_err!(
127	            loc,
128	            TranslationErr::unsupported(format!(
129	                "MMA {fragment_name} fragment must be an array of {expected_len} scalar registers"
130	            ))
131	        );
132	    }
133	
134	    let mut registers = Vec::with_capacity(expected_len);
135	    for index in 0..expected_len {
136	        let extract = Operation::new(
137	            ctx,
138	            MirExtractFieldOp::get_concrete_op_info(),
139	            vec![expected_element_ty],
140	            vec![array],
141	            vec![],
142	            0,
143	        );
144	        extract.deref_mut(ctx).set_loc(loc.clone());
145	        let extract = MirExtractFieldOp::new(extract);
146	        extract.set_attr_index(ctx, FieldIndexAttr(index as u32));
147	        if let Some(previous) = last_op {
148	            extract.get_operation().insert_after(ctx, previous);
149	        } else {
150	            extract.get_operation().insert_at_front(block_ptr, ctx);
151	        }
152	        last_op = Some(extract.get_operation());
153	        registers.push(extract.get_operation().deref(ctx).get_result(0));
154	    }
155	
156	    Ok((registers, last_op.expect("non-empty MMA fragments")))
157	}

159	/// Emit `mma_m16n8k16_f32_bf16` as a register-producing dialect operation.
160	///
161	/// Args:
162	/// - `args[0]`: `[f32; 4]` C accumulator registers
163	/// - `args[1]`: `[u32; 4]` packed A fragment registers
164	/// - `args[2]`: `[u32; 2]` packed B fragment registers
165	///
166	/// Returns: `[f32; 4]` D accumulator registers.
167	pub fn emit_mma_m16n8k16_f32_bf16(
168	    ctx: &mut Context,
169	    body: &mir::Body,
170	    args: &[mir::Operand],
171	    destination: &mir::Place,
172	    target: &Option<usize>,
173	    block_ptr: Ptr<BasicBlock>,
174	    prev_op: Option<Ptr<Operation>>,
175	    value_map: &mut ValueMap,
176	    block_map: &[Ptr<BasicBlock>],
177	    loc: Location,
178	) -> TranslationResult<Ptr<Operation>> {
179	    if args.len() != 3 {
180	        return input_err!(
181	            loc.clone(),
182	            TranslationErr::unsupported(format!(
183	                "mma_m16n8k16_f32_bf16 expects 3 arguments (acc, a, b), got {}",
184	                args.len()
185	            ))
186	        );
187	    }
188	
189	    let f32_ty = FP32Type::get(ctx);
190	    let u32_ty = IntegerType::get(ctx, 32, Signedness::Unsigned);
191	
192	    let (c_array, last_op) = rvalue::translate_operand(
193	        ctx,
194	        body,
195	        &args[0],
196	        value_map,
197	        block_ptr,
198	        prev_op,
199	        loc.clone(),
200	    )?;
201	    let (c_registers, last_op) = extract_array_registers(
202	        ctx,
203	        c_array,
204	        f32_ty.into(),
205	        4,
206	        block_ptr,
207	        last_op,
208	        loc.clone(),
209	        "C",
210	    )?;
211	
212	    let (a_array, last_op_after) = rvalue::translate_operand(
213	        ctx,
214	        body,
215	        &args[1],
216	        value_map,
217	        block_ptr,
218	        Some(last_op),
219	        loc.clone(),
220	    )?;
221	    let (a_registers, last_op) = extract_array_registers(
222	        ctx,
223	        a_array,
224	        u32_ty.into(),
225	        4,
226	        block_ptr,
227	        last_op_after,
228	        loc.clone(),
229	        "A",
230	    )?;
231	
232	    let (b_array, last_op_after) = rvalue::translate_operand(
233	        ctx,
234	        body,
235	        &args[2],
236	        value_map,
237	        block_ptr,
238	        Some(last_op),
239	        loc.clone(),
240	    )?;
241	    let (b_registers, last_op) = extract_array_registers(
242	        ctx,
243	        b_array,
244	        u32_ty.into(),
245	        2,
246	        block_ptr,
247	        last_op_after,
248	        loc.clone(),
249	        "B",
250	    )?;
251	
252	    let mut operands = c_registers;
253	    operands.extend(a_registers);
254	    operands.extend(b_registers);
255	
256	    let mma_op = Operation::new(
257	        ctx,
258	        MmaM16N8K16F32Bf16Op::get_concrete_op_info(),
259	        vec![f32_ty.into(); 4],
260	        operands,
261	        vec![],
262	        0,
263	    );
264	    mma_op.deref_mut(ctx).set_loc(loc.clone());
265	    mma_op.insert_after(ctx, last_op);
266	
267	    let d_registers = (0..4)
268	        .map(|index| mma_op.deref(ctx).get_result(index))
269	        .collect();
270	    let array_ty = MirArrayType::get(ctx, f32_ty.into(), 4);
271	    let d_array = Operation::new(
272	        ctx,
273	        MirConstructArrayOp::get_concrete_op_info(),
274	        vec![array_ty.into()],
275	        d_registers,
276	        vec![],
277	        0,
278	    );
279	    d_array.deref_mut(ctx).set_loc(loc.clone());
280	    d_array.insert_after(ctx, mma_op);
281	    let result = d_array.deref(ctx).get_result(0);
282	
283	    emit_store_result_and_goto(
284	        ctx,
285	        destination,
286	        result,
287	        target,
288	        block_ptr,
289	        d_array,
290	        value_map,
291	        block_map,
292	        loc,
293	        "mma_m16n8k16_f32_bf16 call without target block",
294	    )
295	}

711	/// Emit `mma_m8n8k4_f64`: Warp MMA with f64 accumulator and f64 inputs.
712	///
713	/// Args:
714	/// - arg 0: `[f64; 2]` (lane-local C accumulator fragment)
715	/// - arg 1: `f64` (lane-local A fragment)
716	/// - arg 2: `f64` (lane-local B fragment)
717	///
718	/// Returns: `[f64; 2]` (lane-local D result fragment)
719	#[allow(clippy::too_many_arguments)]
720	pub fn emit_mma_m8n8k4_f64(
721	    ctx: &mut Context,
722	    body: &mir::Body,
723	    args: &[mir::Operand],
724	    destination: &mir::Place,
725	    target: &Option<usize>,
726	    block_ptr: Ptr<BasicBlock>,
727	    prev_op: Option<Ptr<Operation>>,
728	    value_map: &mut ValueMap,
729	    block_map: &[Ptr<BasicBlock>],
730	    loc: Location,
731	) -> TranslationResult<Ptr<Operation>> {
732	    if args.len() != 3 {
733	        return input_err!(
734	            loc.clone(),
735	            TranslationErr::unsupported(format!(
736	                "mma_m8n8k4_f64 expects 3 arguments (acc, a, b), got {}",
737	                args.len()
738	            ))
739	        );
740	    }
741	
742	    let (acc, mut last_op) = rvalue::translate_operand(
743	        ctx,
744	        body,
745	        &args[0],
746	        value_map,
747	        block_ptr,
748	        prev_op,
749	        loc.clone(),
750	    )?;
751	
752	    let f64_ty = FP64Type::get(ctx);
753	    let acc_ty = acc.get_type(ctx);
754	    let valid_acc = acc_ty
755	        .deref(ctx)
756	        .downcast_ref::<MirArrayType>()
757	        .is_some_and(|array| {
758	            array.size() == 2
759	                && array
760	                    .element_type()
761	                    .deref(ctx)
762	                    .downcast_ref::<FP64Type>()
763	                    .is_some()
764	        });
765	    if !valid_acc {
766	        return input_err!(
767	            loc.clone(),
768	            TranslationErr::unsupported(
769	                "mma_m8n8k4_f64 accumulator must have type [f64; 2]".to_string()
770	            )
771	        );
772	    }
773	
774	    let mut accumulator_registers = Vec::with_capacity(2);
775	    for index in 0..2 {
776	        let extract = Operation::new(
777	            ctx,
778	            MirExtractFieldOp::get_concrete_op_info(),
779	            vec![f64_ty.into()],
780	            vec![acc],
781	            vec![],
782	            0,
783	        );
784	        extract.deref_mut(ctx).set_loc(loc.clone());
785	        MirExtractFieldOp::new(extract).set_attr_index(ctx, FieldIndexAttr(index));
786	        if let Some(previous) = last_op {
787	            extract.insert_after(ctx, previous);
788	        } else {
789	            extract.insert_at_front(block_ptr, ctx);
790	        }
791	        last_op = Some(extract);
792	        accumulator_registers.push(extract.deref(ctx).get_result(0));
793	    }
794	
795	    let (a, last_op_after) = rvalue::translate_operand(
796	        ctx,
797	        body,
798	        &args[1],
799	        value_map,
800	        block_ptr,
801	        last_op,
802	        loc.clone(),
803	    )?;
804	    last_op = last_op_after;
805	
806	    let (b, last_op_after) = rvalue::translate_operand(
807	        ctx,
808	        body,
809	        &args[2],
810	        value_map,
811	        block_ptr,
812	        last_op,
813	        loc.clone(),
814	    )?;
815	    last_op = last_op_after;
816	
817	    for (name, value) in [("A", a), ("B", b)] {
818	        if value
819	            .get_type(ctx)
820	            .deref(ctx)
821	            .downcast_ref::<FP64Type>()
822	            .is_none()
823	        {
824	            return input_err!(
825	                loc.clone(),
826	                TranslationErr::unsupported(format!(

828	                ))
829	            );
830	        }
831	    }

833	    let operands = vec![accumulator_registers[0], accumulator_registers[1], a, b];
834	
835	    let mma_op = Operation::new(

838	        vec![f64_ty.into(), f64_ty.into()],
839	        operands,
840	        vec![],
841	        0,
842	    );
843	    mma_op.deref_mut(ctx).set_loc(loc.clone());
844	
845	    if let Some(prev) = last_op {
846	        mma_op.insert_after(ctx, prev);
847	    } else {
848	        mma_op.insert_at_front(block_ptr, ctx);
849	    }
850	
851	    let results: Vec<Value> = (0..2)
852	        .map(|index| mma_op.deref(ctx).get_result(index))
853	        .collect();
854	    let array_ty = MirArrayType::get(ctx, f64_ty.into(), 2);
855	    let array = Operation::new(
856	        ctx,
857	        MirConstructArrayOp::get_concrete_op_info(),
858	        vec![array_ty.into()],
859	        results,
860	        vec![],
861	        0,
862	    );
863	    array.deref_mut(ctx).set_loc(loc.clone());
864	    array.insert_after(ctx, mma_op);
865	    let array_result = array.deref(ctx).get_result(0);
866

868	        ctx,
869	        destination,
870	        array_result,

872	        block_ptr,
873	        array,
874	        value_map,
875	        block_map,
876	        loc,

1	/*
2	 * SPDX-FileCopyrightText: Copyright (c) 2026 NVIDIA CORPORATION & AFFILIATES. All rights reserved.
3	 * SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0
4	 */
5	
6	//! Atomic operation intrinsic handlers.
7	//!
8	//! Translates atomic method calls into NVVM atomic dialect operations.
9	//! Supports two front-ends:
10	//!
11	//! 1. **`cuda_device::atomic::*`** — custom GPU atomic types with explicit scope
12	//! 2. **`core::sync::atomic::*`** — standard library atomics (via `std::intrinsics::atomic_*`)
13	//!
14	//! Both front-ends emit the same NVVM ops and share the entire lowering pipeline
15	//! (mir-lower fence splitting → LLVM dialect → export → llc → PTX).
16	//!
17	//! # cuda_device Path — Type Resolution
18	//!
19	//! The atomic type name encodes scope and element type:
20	//!
21	//! ```text
22	//! BlockAtomicI64::fetch_add
23	//! ─────┬────────  ────┬────
24	//!   scope prefix    method
25	//!       └── AtomicI64 = 64-bit signed integer
26	//! ```
27	//!
28	//! | Prefix            | Scope   | PTX    |
29	//! |-------------------|---------|--------|
30	//! | `DeviceAtomic*`   | Device  | `.gpu` |
31	//! | `BlockAtomic*`    | Block   | `.cta` |
32	//! | `SystemAtomic*`   | System  | `.sys` |
33	//!
34	//! # cuda_device Path — Method → RMW Kind Mapping
35	//!
36	//! | Method       | Integer RMW Kind   | Float RMW Kind |
37	//! |--------------|--------------------|----------------|
38	//! | `fetch_add`  | `Add`              | `FAdd`         |
39	//! | `fetch_sub`  | `Sub`              | `FAdd(-x)`     |
40	//! | `fetch_and`  | `And`              | —              |
41	//! | `fetch_or`   | `Or`               | —              |
42	//! | `fetch_xor`  | `Xor`              | —              |
43	//! | `fetch_min`  | `Min` / `UMin` `[*]` | —            |
44	//! | `fetch_max`  | `Max` / `UMax` `[*]` | —            |
45	//! | `swap`       | `Xchg`             | `Xchg`         |
46	//!
47	//! `[*]` `fetch_min`/`fetch_max` use signed (`Min`/`Max`) for `I32`/`I64`,
48	//!     unsigned (`UMin`/`UMax`) for `U32`/`U64`.
49	//!
50	//! # core::sync::atomic Path
51	//!
52	//! Standard library atomics compile down to `std::intrinsics::atomic_*` (or
53	//! `core::intrinsics::atomic_*` in `#![no_std]`).  These are generic intrinsics
54	//! whose ordering is a **const generic**, not a runtime argument:
55	//!
56	//! ```text
57	//! std::intrinsics::atomic_xadd::<u32, u32, AtomicOrdering::Relaxed>(ptr, val)
58	//! ─────────────────────┬─────    ──┬──      ────────┬───────────── ──┬──  ─┬─
59	//!                 intrinsic name   type          ordering           ptr   val
60	//! ```
61	//!
62	//! All `core::sync::atomic` operations are lowered with **system scope** (`.sys`)
63	//! for safe host-device coherence, matching CUDA C++ `cuda::atomic<T>` defaults.
64	
65	use super::super::helpers::emit_store_result_and_goto;
66	use crate::error::{TranslationErr, TranslationResult};
67	use crate::translator::rvalue;
68	use crate::translator::values::ValueMap;
69	
70	use dialect_nvvm::ops::atomic::{
71	    AtomicOrdering, AtomicRmwKind, AtomicScope, NvvmAtomAddBf16x2Op, NvvmAtomAddF16x2Op,
72	    NvvmAtomicCmpxchgOp, NvvmAtomicLoadOp, NvvmAtomicRmwOp, NvvmAtomicStoreOp,
73	};
74	
75	use dialect_mir::ops::MirNegOp;
76	use dialect_mir::types::MirFP16Type;
77	use pliron::basic_block::BasicBlock;
78	use pliron::builtin::types::{FP32Type, FP64Type, IntegerType, Signedness};
79	use pliron::context::{Context, Ptr};
80	use pliron::input_err;
81	use pliron::location::{Located, Location};
82	use pliron::op::Op;
83	use pliron::operation::Operation;
84	use pliron::r#type::Typed;
85	use rustc_public::mir;
86	use rustc_public::ty::{GenericArgKind, RigidTy, TyConstKind, TyKind};
87	// =============================================================================
88	// Type info — extracted from the atomic type name in the call path
89	// =============================================================================
90	
91	/// Describes an atomic type parsed from a `cuda_device::atomic::*` path.
92	///
93	/// Example: `BlockAtomicI64` → `{ bit_width: 64, is_float: false, is_signed: true, scope: Block }`
94	pub struct AtomicTypeInfo {
95	    pub bit_width: u32,
96	    pub is_float: bool,
97	    pub is_signed: bool,
98	    pub scope: AtomicScope,
99	}
100	
101	impl AtomicTypeInfo {
102	    /// Get the pliron result type for this atomic's element.
103	    fn element_type(&self, ctx: &mut Context) -> pliron::r#type::TypeHandle {
104	        if self.is_float {
105	            match self.bit_width {
106	                // Rust `f16` is represented by dialect-mir's own `mir.fp16` (apfloat::Half);
107	                // f32/f64 reuse the pliron builtin float types.
108	                16 => MirFP16Type::get(ctx).into(),
109	                32 => FP32Type::get(ctx).into(),
110	                64 => FP64Type::get(ctx).into(),
111	                _ => unreachable!("unsupported float atomic width: {}", self.bit_width),
112	            }
113	        } else {
114	            let signedness = if self.is_signed {
115	                Signedness::Signed
116	            } else {
117	                Signedness::Unsigned
118	            };
119	            IntegerType::get(ctx, self.bit_width, signedness).to_handle()
120	        }
121	    }
122	}
123	
124	/// Parse an atomic type name (e.g., `"DeviceAtomicU32"`, `"BlockAtomicI64"`) into type info.
125	///
126	/// Device scope uses the `DeviceAtomic*` prefix to avoid name collision with
127	/// `core::sync::atomic::Atomic*`. Returns `None` if the name doesn't match.
128	fn parse_atomic_type_name(type_name: &str) -> Option<AtomicTypeInfo> {
129	    // Extract scope prefix and base type suffix. Try longer prefixes first.
130	    let (scope, base) = if let Some(rest) = type_name.strip_prefix("BlockAtomic") {

1	/*
2	 * SPDX-FileCopyrightText: Copyright (c) 2026 NVIDIA CORPORATION & AFFILIATES. All rights reserved.
3	 * SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0
4	 */
5	
6	//! Common helper functions for terminator translation.
7	//!
8	//! This module contains utility functions shared across terminator handlers:
9	//!
10	//! - [`emit_goto`]: Unconditional zero-operand branch to a target block.
11	//! - [`emit_store_result_and_goto`]: Write an intrinsic result to the
12	//!   destination local's slot, then branch to the success target.
13	//! - [`emit_function_call`]: General function call emission.
14	//! - [`emit_nvvm_intrinsic`]: Simple NVVM intrinsic emission.
15	//! - [`emit_unit_noop_intrinsic`]: Compiler-hint intrinsics with no codegen effect.
16	//! - [`insert_op`]: Common operation insertion pattern.
17	
18	use crate::error::{TranslationErr, TranslationResult};
19	use crate::translator::rvalue;
20	use crate::translator::values::ValueMap;
21	use dialect_mir::ops::{MirCallOp, MirGotoOp};
22	use pliron::basic_block::BasicBlock;
23	use pliron::builtin::types::{IntegerType, Signedness};
24	use pliron::context::{Context, Ptr};
25	use pliron::input_err;
26	use pliron::location::{Located, Location};
27	use pliron::op::Op;
28	use pliron::operation::Operation;
29	use pliron::value::Value;
30	use rustc_public::mir;
31	
32	/// Emits a zero-operand `mir.goto` to the target block.
33	///
34	/// Non-entry blocks carry no arguments; cross-block data flow travels
35	/// through the per-local alloca slots instead.
36	pub fn emit_goto(
37	    ctx: &mut Context,
38	    target_idx: usize,
39	    prev_op: Ptr<Operation>,
40	    block_map: &[Ptr<BasicBlock>],
41	    loc: Location,
42	) -> Ptr<Operation> {
43	    let target_block = block_map[target_idx];
44	    let goto_op = Operation::new(
45	        ctx,
46	        MirGotoOp::get_concrete_op_info(),
47	        vec![],
48	        vec![],
49	        vec![target_block],
50	        0,
51	    );
52	    goto_op.deref_mut(ctx).set_loc(loc);
53	    goto_op.insert_after(ctx, prev_op);
54	    goto_op
55	}
56	
57	/// Stores `result_value` into `destination`'s slot and emits a branch to
58	/// `target`.
59	///
60	/// Shared "write result + branch to success block" epilogue for intrinsic
61	/// handlers. The store is emitted after `prev_op`; the goto chains after the
62	/// store (or after `prev_op` directly if the destination is a ZST with no
63	/// backing slot). Returns the goto operation.
64	#[allow(clippy::too_many_arguments)]
65	pub fn emit_store_result_and_goto(
66	    ctx: &mut Context,
67	    destination: &mir::Place,
68	    result_value: Value,
69	    target: &Option<usize>,
70	    block_ptr: Ptr<BasicBlock>,
71	    prev_op: Ptr<Operation>,
72	    value_map: &mut ValueMap,
73	    block_map: &[Ptr<BasicBlock>],
74	    loc: Location,
75	    no_target_msg: &str,
76	) -> TranslationResult<Ptr<Operation>> {
77	    let goto_prev = value_map
78	        .store_local(
79	            ctx,
80	            destination.local,
81	            result_value,
82	            block_ptr,
83	            Some(prev_op),
84	        )
85	        .unwrap_or(prev_op);
86	
87	    if let Some(target_idx) = target {
88	        Ok(emit_goto(ctx, *target_idx, goto_prev, block_map, loc))
89	    } else {
90	        input_err!(
91	            loc.clone(),
92	            TranslationErr::unsupported(no_target_msg.to_string())
93	        )
94	    }
95	}
96	
97	/// Inserts an operation after the previous one, or at the front of the block.
98	///
99	/// This is a common pattern used throughout terminator translation.
100	#[inline]
101	pub fn insert_op(
102	    ctx: &mut Context,
103	    op: Ptr<Operation>,
104	    block_ptr: Ptr<BasicBlock>,
105	    prev_op: Option<Ptr<Operation>>,
106	) {
107	    match prev_op {
108	        Some(prev) => op.insert_after(ctx, prev),
109	        None => op.insert_at_front(block_ptr, ctx),
110	    }
111	}
112	
113	/// Emits a regular (non-intrinsic) function call.
114	///
115	/// # Process
116	///
117	/// 1. Translate all MIR arguments to Pliron IR values
118	/// 2. Create a `mir.call` operation carrying the callee's name attribute
119	/// 3. Store the result into the destination local's slot
120	/// 4. Emit a zero-operand goto to the call's success target
121	///
122	/// Reference arguments (`&mut local`) are handed the local's alloca slot
123	/// pointer directly, so callee writes through the reference are observed by
124	/// subsequent loads in the caller without any explicit reload plumbing.
125	#[allow(clippy::too_many_arguments)]
126	pub fn emit_function_call(
127	    ctx: &mut Context,
128	    body: &mir::Body,
129	    callee_name: &str,
130	    args: &[mir::Operand],
131	    destination: &mir::Place,
132	    return_type: pliron::r#type::TypeHandle,
133	    target: &Option<usize>,
134	    block_ptr: Ptr<BasicBlock>,
135	    prev_op: Option<Ptr<Operation>>,
136	    value_map: &mut ValueMap,
137	    block_map: &[Ptr<BasicBlock>],
138	    loc: Location,
139	) -> TranslationResult<Ptr<Operation>> {
140	    let mut arg_values = Vec::new();
141	    let mut last_op = prev_op;
142	
143	    for arg in args {
144	        let (arg_value, arg_last_op) =
145	            rvalue::translate_operand(ctx, body, arg, value_map, block_ptr, last_op, loc.clone())?;
146	        arg_values.push(arg_value);
147	        last_op = arg_last_op;
148	    }
149	
150	    use pliron::builtin::attributes::StringAttr;
151	
152	    let call_op = Operation::new(
153	        ctx,
154	        MirCallOp::get_concrete_op_info(),
155	        vec![return_type],
156	        arg_values,
157	        vec![],
158	        0,
159	    );
160	    call_op.deref_mut(ctx).set_loc(loc.clone());
161	
162	    let callee_attr = StringAttr::new(callee_name.into());
163	    call_op.deref_mut(ctx).attributes.set(
164	        pliron::identifier::Identifier::try_from("callee").unwrap(),
165	        callee_attr,
166	    );
167	
168	    let call_op = if let Some(prev) = last_op {
169	        call_op.insert_after(ctx, prev);
170	        call_op
171	    } else {
172	        call_op.insert_at_front(block_ptr, ctx);
173	        call_op
174	    };
175	
176	    let result_value = call_op.deref(ctx).get_result(0);
177	
178	    let goto_prev = value_map
179	        .store_local(
180	            ctx,
181	            destination.local,
182	            result_value,
183	            block_ptr,
184	            Some(call_op),
185	        )
186	        .unwrap_or(call_op);
187	
188	    if let Some(target_idx) = target {
189	        Ok(emit_goto(ctx, *target_idx, goto_prev, block_map, loc))
190	    } else {
191	        input_err!(
192	            loc.clone(),
193	            TranslationErr::unsupported("Call terminator without target not supported".to_string(),)
194	        )
195	    }
196	}
197	
198	/// Emits a simple NVVM intrinsic that takes no operands and returns `u32`.
199	///
200	/// Used for thread/block position intrinsics:
201	/// - `ReadPtxSregTidX/Y` (threadIdx.x/y)
202	/// - `ReadPtxSregCtaidX/Y` (blockIdx.x/y)
203	/// - `ReadPtxSregNtidX/Y` (blockDim.x/y)
204	/// - `ReadPtxSregLaneId` (lane_id)
205	/// - `ReadPtxSregLanemaskLt/Le/Eq/Ge/Gt` (lane-position masks)
206	#[allow(clippy::too_many_arguments)]
207	pub fn emit_nvvm_intrinsic(
208	    ctx: &mut Context,
209	    opid: (
210	        fn(pliron::context::Ptr<pliron::operation::Operation>) -> pliron::op::OpObj,
211	        std::any::TypeId,
212	    ),
213	    destination: &mir::Place,
214	    target: &Option<usize>,
215	    block_ptr: Ptr<BasicBlock>,
216	    prev_op: Option<Ptr<Operation>>,
217	    value_map: &mut ValueMap,
218	    block_map: &[Ptr<BasicBlock>],
219	    loc: Location,
220	) -> TranslationResult<Ptr<Operation>> {
221	    emit_nvvm_integer_intrinsic(
222	        ctx,
223	        opid,
224	        32,
225	        destination,
226	        target,
227	        block_ptr,
228	        prev_op,
229	        value_map,
230	        block_map,
231	        loc,
232	    )
233	}
234	
235	/// Emits a zero-operand NVVM operation returning the full 64-bit PTX value.
236	#[allow(clippy::too_many_arguments)]
237	pub fn emit_nvvm_intrinsic_u64(
238	    ctx: &mut Context,
239	    opid: (
240	        fn(pliron::context::Ptr<pliron::operation::Operation>) -> pliron::op::OpObj,
241	        std::any::TypeId,
242	    ),
243	    destination: &mir::Place,
244	    target: &Option<usize>,
245	    block_ptr: Ptr<BasicBlock>,
246	    prev_op: Option<Ptr<Operation>>,
247	    value_map: &mut ValueMap,
248	    block_map: &[Ptr<BasicBlock>],
249	    loc: Location,
250	) -> TranslationResult<Ptr<Operation>> {
251	    emit_nvvm_integer_intrinsic(
252	        ctx,
253	        opid,
254	        64,
255	        destination,
256	        target,
257	        block_ptr,
258	        prev_op,
259	        value_map,
260	        block_map,
261	        loc,
262	    )
263	}
264	
265	#[allow(clippy::too_many_arguments)]
266	fn emit_nvvm_integer_intrinsic(
267	    ctx: &mut Context,
268	    opid: (
269	        fn(pliron::context::Ptr<pliron::operation::Operation>) -> pliron::op::OpObj,
270	        std::any::TypeId,
271	    ),
272	    result_width: u32,
273	    destination: &mir::Place,
274	    target: &Option<usize>,
275	    block_ptr: Ptr<BasicBlock>,
276	    prev_op: Option<Ptr<Operation>>,
277	    value_map: &mut ValueMap,
278	    block_map: &[Ptr<BasicBlock>],
279	    loc: Location,
280	) -> TranslationResult<Ptr<Operation>> {
281	    let result_type = IntegerType::get(ctx, result_width, Signedness::Unsigned);
282	
283	    let nvvm_op = Operation::new(ctx, opid, vec![result_type.to_handle()], vec![], vec![], 0);
284	    nvvm_op.deref_mut(ctx).set_loc(loc.clone());
285	
286	    let last_op = if let Some(prev) = prev_op {
287	        nvvm_op.insert_after(ctx, prev);
288	        nvvm_op
289	    } else {
290	        nvvm_op.insert_at_front(block_ptr, ctx);
291	        nvvm_op
292	    };
293	
294	    let result_value = nvvm_op.deref(ctx).get_result(0);
295	
296	    let goto_prev = value_map
297	        .store_local(
298	            ctx,
299	            destination.local,
300	            result_value,
301	            block_ptr,
302	            Some(last_op),
303	        )
304	        .unwrap_or(last_op);
305	
306	    if let Some(target_idx) = target {
307	        Ok(emit_goto(ctx, *target_idx, goto_prev, block_map, loc))
308	    } else {
309	        input_err!(
310	            loc.clone(),
311	            TranslationErr::unsupported("Call terminator without target not supported".to_string(),)
312	        )
313	    }
314	}
315	
316	/// Emits a unit-returning intrinsic that has no codegen effect on GPU.
317	///
318	/// Used for compiler-hint intrinsics like `core::intrinsics::cold_path` whose
319	/// semantics are purely advisory. We materialize a unit value for the MIR
320	/// destination and continue to the target block without emitting a real call.
321	#[allow(clippy::too_many_arguments)]
322	pub fn emit_unit_noop_intrinsic(
323	    ctx: &mut Context,
324	    destination: &mir::Place,
325	    target: &Option<usize>,
326	    block_ptr: Ptr<BasicBlock>,
327	    prev_op: Option<Ptr<Operation>>,
328	    value_map: &mut ValueMap,
329	    block_map: &[Ptr<BasicBlock>],
330	    loc: Location,
331	    intrinsic_name: &str,
332	) -> TranslationResult<Ptr<Operation>> {
333	    let unit_ty = dialect_mir::types::MirTupleType::get(ctx, vec![]);
334	    let unit_op = Operation::new(
335	        ctx,
336	        dialect_mir::ops::MirConstructTupleOp::get_concrete_op_info(),
337	        vec![unit_ty.into()],
338	        vec![],
339	        vec![],
340	        0,
341	    );
342	    unit_op.deref_mut(ctx).set_loc(loc.clone());
343	    insert_op(ctx, unit_op, block_ptr, prev_op);
344	
345	    let unit_val = unit_op.deref(ctx).get_result(0);
346	    let goto_prev = value_map
347	        .store_local(ctx, destination.local, unit_val, block_ptr, Some(unit_op))
348	        .unwrap_or(unit_op);
349	
350	    if let Some(target_idx) = target {
351	        Ok(emit_goto(ctx, *target_idx, goto_prev, block_map, loc))
352	    } else {
353	        input_err!(
354	            loc.clone(),
355	            TranslationErr::unsupported(format!(
356	                "{} call without target not supported",
357	                intrinsic_name
358	            ))
359	        )
360	    }
361	}
362
